concepts.name: Action Potential
concepts.def: The electrical signal that travels along an axon. Triggered when membrane reaches threshold (~-55mV); Na+ rushes in causing depolarization, K+ rushes out causing repolarization. All-or-none phenomenon.

concepts.name: Adult Neurogenesis
concepts.def: Production of new neurons in the adult brain. Two regions: subventricular zone (SVZ) -> olfactory bulb; hippocampal dentate gyrus. New hippocampal neurons may contribute to pattern separation and contextual memory encoding. Stress suppresses; aerobic exercise promotes. BDNF mediates exercise-induced neurogenesis.
concepts.keywords: dentate gyrus, subventricular zone, olfactory bulb, pattern separation, BDNF, exercise, stress, contextual learning, adult brain

concepts.name: Agoraphobia
concepts.def: Fear and avoidance of situations where escape may be difficult or help unavailable during a panic attack; can exist with or without panic disorder in DSM-5.
concepts.keywords: agoraphobia, avoidance, panic, DSM-5 separate diagnosis, exposure therapy, CBT

concepts.name: Alcohol Use Disorder
concepts.def: Problematic alcohol use causing clinically significant impairment; genetic, neurobiological, and social etiology; treated with AA, CBT, naltrexone, acamprosate, disulfiram (Antabuse).
concepts.keywords: alcohol, DSM-5, dependence, withdrawal, delirium tremens, naltrexone, acamprosate, Antabuse, controlled drinking

concepts.def: 12-step self-help group for alcohol use disorder; views alcoholism as a disease requiring abstinence and peer support.
concepts.keywords: AA, 12-step, abstinence, self-help, alcohol use disorder

concepts.name: Alzheimer's Disease
concepts.def: Most common dementia; amyloid plaques, neurofibrillary tangles, neuronal loss; APOE-4 allele risk; progressive memory and cognitive decline; no cure but some symptomatic treatments.
concepts.keywords: Alzheimer's, dementia, plaques, tangles, APOE-4, acetylcholine, hippocampus, late life, neurocognitive

concepts.name: Alzheimer's Disease — Neuroscience Perspective
concepts.def: Most common dementia. Pathology: extracellular amyloid plaques (beta-amyloid from APP cleavage) and intracellular neurofibrillary tangles (hyperphosphorylated tau). Begins in entorhinal cortex, spreads to hippocampus then association cortex. ApoE-4 allele = genetic risk. PiB-PET detects amyloid in vivo.
concepts.keywords: amyloid plaques, neurofibrillary tangles, tau, beta-amyloid, ApoE-4, entorhinal cortex, hippocampus, PiB-PET, acetylcholine, APOE gene
concepts.mnemonic: Alzheimer's = PLAQUE (amyloid outside) and TANGLE (tau inside). Starts in hippocampus: first symptom is memory loss.

concepts.name: Amygdala
concepts.def: Almond-shaped subcortical structure in medial temporal lobe. Key roles: fear conditioning (lateral nucleus), emotional memory modulation (basolateral complex), emotional expression (central nucleus). Receives high and low road inputs for threat detection. Case study S.M.: bilateral amygdala damage = no fear.
concepts.keywords: fear conditioning, S.M. case, emotional memory, threat detection, high road low road, basolateral complex, Urbach-Wiethe, face perception
concepts.mnemonic: Amygdala = ALARM system. Sounds emergency for fear, threat, and emotionally important stimuli.

concepts.name: Amygdala
concepts.def: Temporal lobe limbic structure; central to fear conditioning and threat processing; hyperactive in anxiety disorders, PTSD, BPD; hypoactive in psychopathy; key to mood and ASD research.
concepts.keywords: amygdala, fear circuit, limbic, PTSD, anxiety, BPD, autism, psychopathy, mood disorders, threat

concepts.name: Amygdala
concepts.keywords: fear, emotional learning, brain, amygdala

concepts.name: Amygdala & Fear Conditioning
concepts.def: LeDoux identified amygdala as critical for fear conditioning and emotional learning. TWO pathways: Low road (thalamus -> amygdala) = fast automatic threat response. High road (thalamus -> cortex -> amygdala) = slower, cognitively mediated. Basolateral complex processes CS-US associations; central nucleus drives fear responses.
concepts.keywords: LeDoux, CS, US, fear conditioning, basolateral amygdala, central nucleus, low road, high road, thalamus, cortex, implicit emotional learning, PTSD
concepts.mnemonic: Fear takes TWO roads: Fast LOW road (thalamus directly) and Slow HIGH road (via cortex). Low road = emergency bypass. High road = thinking about it.
concepts.theorists: Joseph LeDoux, James, Lange, Cannon, Bard

concepts.name: Anal Stage of Development
concepts.keywords: Freud, psychosexual stages, anal personality

concepts.name: Anchoring
concepts.keywords: social-cognitive theory, judgment, bias, expectancies

concepts.name: Anorexia Nervosa
concepts.def: Restriction of energy intake, intense fear of weight gain, disturbed body image; subtypes: restricting and binge-purge; highest mortality of any psychiatric disorder.
concepts.keywords: anorexia, restricting, binge-purge, amenorrhea, BMI, body image, highest mortality, CBT, family therapy

concepts.name: Anterior Cingulate Cortex (ACC)
concepts.def: Part of medial frontal cortex. Functions: conflict monitoring (detects when competing responses activate), error detection (ERN), pain processing, emotional regulation, attention allocation. Generates error-related negativity (ERN). Key node in cognitive control networks.
concepts.keywords: conflict monitoring, error detection, ERN, feedback-related negativity, pain, emotion regulation, Stroop task, response conflict, medial frontal
concepts.mnemonic: ACC = Alarm/Conflict Center. Rings a bell when there's CONFLICT between competing responses (like Stroop: red ink says BLUE).

concepts.name: Anterograde vs Retrograde Amnesia
concepts.def: Anterograde amnesia: inability to form NEW memories after brain damage. Retrograde amnesia: inability to recall memories from BEFORE the damage. H.M. had severe anterograde but some retrograde. Ribot's law: older memories more resistant (temporally graded retrograde).
concepts.keywords: H.M., anterograde, retrograde, Ribot's law, temporally graded, hippocampus, medial temporal lobe, consolidation
concepts.mnemonic: Anterograde = can't go FORWARD (no new memories). Retrograde = can't go BACK (lost old memories). H.M. mainly anterograde.

concepts.name: Antidepressants (SSRIs, SNRIs, TCAs, MAOIs)
concepts.def: Medications that increase availability of serotonin, norepinephrine, and/or dopamine; first-line for MDD, anxiety disorders, OCD, PTSD, eating disorders.
concepts.keywords: SSRI, SNRI, TCA, MAOI, Prozac, fluoxetine, Zoloft, sertraline, Effexor, tricyclic, monoamine

concepts.name: Antipsychotic Drugs (Neuroleptics)
concepts.def: Medications that block dopamine D2 receptors; 1st-generation (e.g., haloperidol, chlorpromazine) and 2nd-generation (e.g., clozapine, risperidone, olanzapine); primary treatment for schizophrenia.
concepts.keywords: antipsychotic, neuroleptic, haloperidol, clozapine, risperidone, olanzapine, D2, schizophrenia, tardive dyskinesia, extrapyramidal

concepts.name: Antisocial Personality Disorder (APD) & Psychopathy
concepts.def: Pervasive pattern of disregard for and violation of others' rights; psychopathy adds emotional callousness, fearlessness, impulsivity; etiology involves genetics, family, neurobiological factors.
concepts.keywords: APD, antisocial, psychopathy, PCL-R, callousness, conduct disorder precursor, amygdala, fearlessness

concepts.name: Anxiety (Psychoanalytic)
concepts.keywords: psychoanalysis, anxiety, defense mechanisms, free-floating anxiety

concepts.name: Aphasia Types Summary
concepts.def: Broca's: non-fluent, agrammatic, good comprehension, poor repetition (left IFG). Wernicke's: fluent/nonsensical, poor comprehension, poor repetition (left STG). Conduction: fluent, good comprehension, poor repetition (arcuate fasciculus). Global: all modalities impaired. Anomia: word-finding difficulty.
concepts.keywords: Broca's, Wernicke's, conduction, global aphasia, anomia, fluency, comprehension, repetition, left hemisphere, Wernicke-Lichtheim model
concepts.mnemonic: Aphasia = BWCG-A: Broca(broken output), Wernicke(word salad), Conduction(can't repeat), Global(everything), Anomia(name-finding). Match to fluency + comprehension + repetition.

concepts.name: Archetypes
concepts.keywords: Jung, collective unconscious, archetypes

concepts.name: Attachment Behavioral System (ABS)
concepts.keywords: attachment, ABS, Bowlby, infant

concepts.name: Attachment Theory
concepts.def: Bowlby's theory that early caregiver bonds form internal working models shaping adult relationships and mental health; disrupted attachment linked to depression and personality disorders.
concepts.keywords: attachment, Bowlby, Ainsworth, secure, insecure, internal working model, BPD, depression, interpersonal

concepts.name: Attachment Theory
concepts.keywords: Bowlby, secure, anxious-ambivalent, anxious-avoidant, strange situation, internal working models

concepts.name: Attachment Theory (Bowlby & Ainsworth)
concepts.def: Bowlby: Early caregiver-infant bond as biological necessity; internal working models form templates for future relationships. Ainsworth Strange Situation: secure, anxious-ambivalent, avoidant, disorganized attachment styles.
concepts.keywords: Bowlby, Ainsworth, secure base, internal working model, Strange Situation, attachment styles, separation anxiety, BPD
concepts.mnemonic: 4 styles: Secure, Anxious, Avoidant, Disorganized = SAAD when insecure. Strange Situation shows them.

concepts.name: Attachment Theory (Bowlby & Ainsworth)
concepts.def: Evolved system keeping infants near caregivers. 4 types (Strange Situation): Secure, Avoidant, Ambivalent, Disorganized. IWM guides future relationships.
concepts.keywords: Strange Situation, secure attachment, avoidant, ambivalent, disorganized, internal working model, IWM, ethological theory, Bowlby, Ainsworth
concepts.mnemonic: SAAD = Secure (soothed), Anxious-Ambivalent (Angry-distressed), Avoidant (Away), Disorganized (Dazed)

concepts.name: Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD)
concepts.def: A neurodevelopmental disorder defined by impairing levels of inattention, disorganization, and/or hyperactivity-impulsivity inconsistent with developmental level.
concepts.keywords: ADHD, inattention, hyperactivity, impulsivity, two settings, age 12, Combined, Predominantly Inattentive
concepts.mnemonic: 6 before 12, in 2 places - 6 symptoms (5 if adult), onset before age 12, 2+ settings

concepts.name: Attentional Networks (Posner & Petersen 1990)
concepts.def: Three separable attentional systems: Alerting (readiness), Orienting (sensory selection), Executive (conflict resolution/monitoring).
concepts.keywords: alerting, orienting, executive attention, anterior cingulate, parietal, attentional networks, Posner, Stroop, ANT
concepts.mnemonic: AOE = Alerting (ready?), Orienting (where?), Executive (which response?)

concepts.name: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD)
concepts.def: DSM-5: Two core domains - (A) Deficits in social communication and interaction, (B) Restricted, repetitive behaviors. Merged Asperger disorder into ASD. Specifiers: with or without intellectual impairment, severity levels 1-3.
concepts.keywords: joint attention, theory of mind, echolalia, pronoun reversal, restricted interests, Kanner, Lovaas, ABA, Asperger merged
concepts.mnemonic: ASD = SOCIAL deficits + SAMENESS (restricted/repetitive). DSM-5 merged Asperger IN. Two domains only.
concepts.theorists: Leo Kanner, Hans Asperger, Ivar Lovaas

concepts.name: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD)
concepts.def: Neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by social-communication deficits, restricted/repetitive behaviors; spectrum of severity; genetic etiology; behavioral treatments (Lovaas/ABA) most effective.
concepts.keywords: ASD, autism, social communication, repetitive, ABA, Lovaas, applied behavior analysis, genetics, DSM-5, Asperger's

concepts.name: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD)
concepts.def: A neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by persistent deficits in social communication and social interaction, plus restricted, repetitive patterns of behavior, interests, or activities.
concepts.keywords: ASD, social communication, restricted repetitive behavior, Asperger's, spectrum, nonverbal, sensory, echolalia
concepts.mnemonic: SOCIAL + RRBs - BOTH required. No RRBs = not ASD (may be Social Pragmatic Disorder)

concepts.name: Avoidant Personality Disorder
concepts.def: Social inhibition, feelings of inadequacy, hypersensitivity to negative evaluation; desires relationships but avoids them; overlaps with social anxiety disorder; treated with CBT, SSRIs.
concepts.keywords: avoidant personality disorder, social inhibition, inadequacy, negative evaluation, social anxiety overlap, Cluster C, CBT

concepts.name: Bandura, Albert
concepts.keywords: social-cognitive theory, self-efficacy, observational learning, modeling

concepts.name: Basal Ganglia
concepts.def: Subcortical nuclei: striatum (caudate + putamen), globus pallidus, subthalamic nucleus, substantia nigra. Functions: motor control (movement initiation/gating), procedural learning, reward (via dopamine). Disinhibition model: GPi inhibits thalamus; dopamine disinhibits motor plans. Parkinson's: dopamine loss.
concepts.keywords: striatum, caudate, putamen, globus pallidus, substantia nigra, dopamine, Parkinson's, Huntington's, procedural learning, movement gating
concepts.mnemonic: Basal Ganglia = GATEKEEPER. They gate which actions GO and which STOP. Parkinson's = gate is stuck (too much inhibition).

concepts.name: Beck's Cognitive Triad
concepts.def: Beck's model of depression: negative views of (1) the self, (2) the world/environment, and (3) the future. Maintained by cognitive distortions (overgeneralization, catastrophizing, all-or-nothing thinking).
concepts.keywords: Beck, negative automatic thoughts, schema, cognitive distortions, hopelessness, depression, cognitive therapy
concepts.mnemonic: Triad = Self (worthless) + World (hostile) + Future (hopeless). Three S-W-F negatives.

concepts.name: Beck's Cognitive Triad
concepts.def: Aaron Beck's model of depression: negative views of self, world, and future; central to cognitive therapy which targets automatic negative thoughts and maladaptive schemas.
concepts.keywords: Beck, cognitive triad, negative self, world, future, automatic thoughts, schemas, cognitive therapy, depression

concepts.name: Beck, Aaron
concepts.keywords: cognitive therapy, depression, cognitive triad, CBT

concepts.name: Behavioral Activation (BA) Therapy
concepts.def: Behavioral treatment for depression; increases engagement with rewarding activities to counteract withdrawal and anhedonia; derived from operant conditioning principles.
concepts.keywords: behavioral activation, BA, depression, operant, reward, anhedonia, activity scheduling, Lewinsohn

concepts.name: Behavioral Avoidance Test (BAT)
concepts.keywords: BAT, phobia, behavioral assessment, anxiety

concepts.name: Behavioral Genetics
concepts.keywords: heritability, twin studies, adoption studies, genes, nature-nurture

concepts.name: Behavioral Signatures
concepts.keywords: Mischel, CAPS, if-then patterns, situational specificity

concepts.name: Behaviorism
concepts.def: Psychological paradigm emphasizing observable behavior and learning principles (classical/operant conditioning) rather than internal mental states; Watson, Skinner, Pavlov.
concepts.keywords: behaviorism, Watson, Skinner, Pavlov, classical conditioning, operant conditioning, stimulus-response
concepts.theorists: John B. Watson, B.F. Skinner, Ivan Pavlov

concepts.name: Behaviorism
concepts.keywords: conditioning, Skinner, Watson, determinism, observable behavior, environmental determinism

concepts.name: Benzodiazepines
concepts.def: Anxiolytic medications that enhance GABA activity; effective for acute anxiety but risk of dependence and tolerance; examples: alprazolam (Xanax), diazepam, lorazepam.
concepts.keywords: benzodiazepine, GABA, Xanax, alprazolam, anxiolytic, tolerance, dependence, anxiety treatment

concepts.keywords: OCEAN, openness, conscientiousness, extraversion, agreeableness, neuroticism, NEO-PI-R

concepts.name: Binge Eating Disorder (BED)
concepts.def: Recurrent binge eating episodes without compensatory behaviors; associated with distress, obesity risk; treated with CBT, interpersonal therapy, and medications.
concepts.keywords: binge eating disorder, BED, DSM-5, no purging, CBT, IPT, obesity, negative affect

concepts.name: Biopsychosocial Model & Diathesis-Stress Model
concepts.zh: 生物心理社會模型／素質壓力模型
concepts.def: The biopsychosocial model (Engel, 1977) proposes mental disorders arise from interaction of biological, psychological, and social factors. Diathesis-stress model: vulnerability + stressor = disorder onset.
concepts.keywords: biopsychosocial, Engel, diathesis-stress, cognitive triad, Beck, Bowlby, attachment, vulnerability, stress model, case formulation
concepts.mnemonic: BPS = Bio+Psycho+Social = always complete picture. Diathesis-Stress: Vulnerability + Stress threshold = Episode onset
concepts.theorists: George Engel (biopsychosocial), Aaron Beck (cognitive model, CBT), Bowlby (attachment), Zubin & Spring (stress-vulnerability)

concepts.name: Bipolar Disorder
concepts.def: Mood disorder with manic (Bipolar I) or hypomanic (Bipolar II) episodes; often cycling with depression; high heritability; treated with lithium, anticonvulsants, antipsychotics, psychoeducation.
concepts.keywords: bipolar, mania, hypomania, cycling, lithium, mood stabilizer, Bipolar I vs II, heritability, psychoeducation, FFT

concepts.name: Bipolar I and II Disorders
concepts.def: Bipolar I: at least one manic episode. Bipolar II: at least one hypomanic + one MDE, no manic episode. Both involve episodic mood elevation and depression.
concepts.keywords: bipolar, manic episode, hypomanic episode, DIG FAST, 7 days, 4 days, grandiosity, decreased sleep, cyclothymia
concepts.mnemonic: DIG FAST: Distractibility, Inappropriate behavior, Grandiosity, Flight of ideas, Activity increased, Sleep decreased, Talkative
concepts.page: Section II - Bipolar and Related Disorders

concepts.name: Blindsight
concepts.def: Patients with V1 damage have no conscious visual perception in the blind field, yet perform above chance on visual tasks (detection, localization, discrimination) without awareness. Mediated by subcortical pathways (superior colliculus -> pulvinar -> parietal). Key case: D.B. (Weiskrantz). Demonstrates nonconscious visual processing.
concepts.keywords: V1 damage, unconscious vision, superior colliculus, pulvinar, visual awareness, D.B., forced choice, nonconscious processing, Weiskrantz
concepts.mnemonic: Blindsight = Seeing without knowing you're seeing. V1 gone but subcortical routes still work. Guess correctly while insisting you're blind.

concepts.name: Body Dysmorphic Disorder (BDD)
concepts.def: Preoccupation with perceived defects in physical appearance; classified under OCD-related disorders; often comorbid with depression; treated with CBT and SSRIs.
concepts.keywords: BDD, body dysmorphic, OCD-spectrum, appearance, preoccupation, SSRI, CBT, insight

concepts.name: Borderline Personality Disorder (BPD)
concepts.def: Pervasive instability in mood, identity, relationships, and behavior. 9 criteria need 5+: frantic fear of abandonment, unstable relationships, identity disturbance, impulsivity, suicidal/self-harm behavior, emotional instability, emptiness, anger, dissociation.
concepts.keywords: Linehan, DBT, biosocial theory, splitting, identity disturbance, self-harm, impulsivity, abandonment, emotional dysregulation
concepts.mnemonic: BPD: PRAISE - Paranoia/dissociation, Relationships unstable, Abandonment fear, Impulsivity, Self-harm, Emotional instability.

concepts.name: Borderline Personality Disorder (BPD)
concepts.def: Pattern of instability in relationships, self-image, and emotions; fear of abandonment; impulsivity; self-harm; treated with DBT (Dialectical Behavior Therapy).
concepts.keywords: BPD, borderline, DBT, Linehan, impulsivity, self-harm, splitting, abandonment, emotional dysregulation

concepts.name: Brain Plasticity
concepts.keywords: neuroplasticity, brain, learning, serotonin, adaptation

concepts.name: Brain-Machine Interface (BMI)
concepts.def: Direct communication pathway between the brain and external devices. Motor BMIs record from motor cortex to control robotic arms/computer cursors. Patient cases: M.N. (BrainGate), N. Ramsey. Cochlear implants and retinal implants are sensory BMIs. Future potential for paralyzed patients.
concepts.keywords: BrainGate, motor cortex recording, prosthetic limb, paralysis, cochlear implant, retinal implant, neural prosthetics, population vector
concepts.mnemonic: BMI = Brain Makes It work. Record motor cortex signals -> decode movement intention -> command robot arm. The brain controls without moving the body.

concepts.name: Broaden-and-Build Theory
concepts.keywords: positive emotions, Fredrickson, broaden-and-build, well-being

concepts.name: Broca's Area
concepts.def: Left inferior frontal gyrus (BA 44/45). Involved in speech production, syntactic processing, and working memory. Damage causes Broca's aphasia: non-fluent, agrammatic speech with intact comprehension. Leborgne (Tan) was Broca's landmark patient.
concepts.keywords: BA 44, BA 45, speech production, Broca's aphasia, agrammatism, non-fluent, left hemisphere, syntax, Leborgne, Tan
concepts.mnemonic: Broca's = BROKEN production. Speaks in telegraphic bursts. BA 44/45 left IFG. Tan could only say Tan.

concepts.name: Broca's Area and Wernicke's Area
concepts.def: Broca's area (BA44/45, left IFG): speech production and syntax. Damage = Broca's aphasia: non-fluent, effortful speech, intact comprehension, telegraphic output. Wernicke's area (BA22, posterior STG): speech comprehension. Damage = Wernicke's aphasia: fluent but meaningless speech (word salad), poor comprehension. Connected by arcuate fasciculus.
concepts.keywords: Broca aphasia, Wernicke aphasia, arcuate fasciculus, conduction aphasia, anomia, left hemisphere, IFG, posterior STG, Tan (Leborgne), localization
concepts.mnemonic: BROCA = BROken speech (non-fluent). WERNICKE = Word salad (fluent nonsense). Connect them = arcuate fasciculus. Cut the connection = conduction aphasia.

concepts.name: Bronfenbrenner's Ecological Systems Theory
concepts.def: Human development occurs within nested systems: Microsystem, Mesosystem, Exosystem, Macrosystem, Chronosystem.
concepts.keywords: microsystem, mesosystem, exosystem, macrosystem, chronosystem, ecological systems, Bronfenbrenner, proximal processes, cohort effect
concepts.mnemonic: My Mom Eats Mangoes Cheerfully = Micro, Meso, Exo, Macro, Chrono

concepts.name: Bulimia Nervosa
concepts.def: Recurrent binge eating followed by compensatory behaviors (purging, fasting, exercise); normal to slightly elevated weight; CBT most effective; SSRIs as adjunct.
concepts.keywords: bulimia, binge, purge, compensatory behavior, CBT, SSRIs, electrolyte imbalance, Russell's sign, normal weight

concepts.name: CAPS (Cognitive-Affective Processing Systems)
concepts.keywords: Mischel, CAPS, cognitive-affective, personality system, behavioral signatures

concepts.name: Cardinal Traits (Allport)
concepts.keywords: Allport, cardinal traits, dominant disposition, personality structure

concepts.def: In-depth investigation of a single individual; useful for generating/disproving hypotheses and describing rare phenomena; limited external validity and causality.

concepts.name: Cerebellum
concepts.def: Hindbrain structure with 50% of all brain neurons. Functions: timing and coordination of movement, motor learning and adaptation, error-based learning via forward models. Purkinje cells receive climbing fiber error signals. Damage causes ataxia. Also contributes to cognitive timing.
concepts.keywords: Purkinje cells, climbing fibers, ataxia, forward model, motor learning, sensorimotor adaptation, error-based learning, timing
concepts.mnemonic: Cerebellum = Error Corrector. Like a GPS recalculating: compares planned vs actual movement and fixes mistakes.

concepts.name: Civil Commitment
concepts.def: Involuntary hospitalization of individuals with mental illness who are dangerous to self or others; requires imminent dangerousness; distinct from criminal commitment.
concepts.keywords: civil commitment, involuntary hospitalization, dangerousness, Wyatt v. Stickney, O'Connor v. Donaldson, rights, mental illness

concepts.name: Classical Conditioning
concepts.def: Pavlov's learning paradigm: neutral stimulus paired with unconditioned stimulus elicits conditioned response; foundation of behavioral models of phobias and anxiety.
concepts.keywords: classical conditioning, Pavlov, CS, US, CR, UR, acquisition, extinction, Little Albert, phobia etiology

concepts.name: Classical Conditioning
concepts.keywords: Pavlov, conditioned stimulus, unconditioned stimulus, extinction, generalization, discrimination

concepts.name: Classical Conditioning (Pavlov & Watson)
concepts.def: Learning through association. UCS produces UCR; neutral CS paired with UCS eventually evokes CR. Extinction, spontaneous recovery, generalization, discrimination. Watson's Little Albert: fear acquisition. Basis for phobia treatment.
concepts.keywords: Pavlov, Little Albert, Watson, UCS UCR CS CR, extinction, generalization, counterconditioning, fear acquisition, aversive conditioning
concepts.mnemonic: Pavlov's dog: BELL (CS) + FOOD (UCS) = DROOL (UCR) -> BELL alone = DROOL (CR). Neutral becomes conditioned.
concepts.theorists: Ivan Pavlov, John Watson, Rosalie Rayner

concepts.name: Client-Centered Therapy
concepts.keywords: Rogers, person-centered, unconditional positive regard, empathy, therapeutic conditions

concepts.name: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT)
concepts.def: Structured, time-limited psychotherapy combining Beck's cognitive therapy with behavioral techniques. Gold standard for anxiety, depression, and many disorders. Challenges negative automatic thoughts and schemas.
concepts.keywords: cognitive restructuring, automatic thoughts, schema, Beck, exposure, homework, relapse prevention
concepts.mnemonic: CBT = Change Beliefs + Test Them. Beck's triangle: thoughts-feelings-behaviors

concepts.name: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT)
concepts.def: Integrates behavioral techniques (exposure, reinforcement) with cognitive restructuring; empirically supported for depression, anxiety, OCD, eating disorders, schizophrenia, substance use, and more.
concepts.keywords: CBT, cognitive behavioral, Beck, Ellis, exposure, restructuring, homework, schema, empirically supported, gold standard

concepts.name: Cognitive Control and Executive Function
concepts.def: Top-down regulation of thought and behavior for goal-directed action. Key processes: working memory maintenance, task-switching, inhibitory control, conflict monitoring. Prefrontal cortex (especially dlPFC and ACC) is central. Deficits in ADHD, schizophrenia, OCD, addiction, frontal lobe damage.
concepts.keywords: executive function, working memory, task-switching, inhibitory control, PFC, dlPFC, ACC, Stroop, go/no-go, frontal lobe

concepts.name: Cognitive Therapy for Depression (Beck)
concepts.keywords: Beck, cognitive triad, depression, faulty cognitions, CBT, cognitive-behavioral therapy

concepts.keywords: unconscious, cognitive, implicit processing, motivated unconscious

concepts.name: Collective Unconscious
concepts.keywords: Jung, collective unconscious, archetypes, shared human experience

concepts.name: Competency to Stand Trial
concepts.def: Legal standard requiring defendant to understand nature of criminal proceedings and assist in own defense; assessed by mental health professionals; distinct from insanity defense.
concepts.keywords: competency, stand trial, Dusky standard, Pate v. Robinson, Jackson v. Indiana, adjudicative competence

concepts.name: Conditions of Worth
concepts.keywords: Rogers, conditions of worth, conditional positive regard, self-concept, incongruence

concepts.name: Conduct Disorder
concepts.def: Repetitive pattern of violating rights of others or societal rules; precursor to APD; associated with poor family environment, genetics, neurobiological factors; treated with MST, PMT.
concepts.keywords: conduct disorder, CD, DSM-5, APD precursor, aggression, MST, PMT, callous-unemotional, adolescent-onset, childhood-onset

concepts.name: Conduction Aphasia
concepts.def: Aphasia type characterized by fluent speech with good comprehension but severe impairment in repetition (cannot repeat spoken words). Caused by damage to arcuate fasciculus disconnecting Wernicke's from Broca's area. Frequently makes phonemic paraphasias. Confirms dissociation between comprehension and repetition.
concepts.keywords: arcuate fasciculus, repetition deficit, paraphasias, phonemic, fluent speech, good comprehension, Wernicke-Broca disconnection
concepts.mnemonic: Conduction Aphasia = CONDUCTING signal is broken (arcuate fasciculus). Understands and speaks fluently but can't REPEAT. Cuts the phone line between the two language areas.

concepts.name: Consciousness — Neural Correlates
concepts.def: Hard problem: why does physical brain produce subjective experience (qualia)? Neural correlates of consciousness (NCCs): widespread cortical activity + frontal-parietal networks. Global Workspace Theory (Baars/Dehaene): conscious = information broadcast widely across brain. Locked-in syndrome shows dissociation between consciousness and motor output.
concepts.keywords: hard problem, qualia, NCC, global workspace theory, locked-in syndrome, vegetative state, minimally conscious state, frontal-parietal, Tononi, IIT

concepts.name: Construct System (Kelly)
concepts.keywords: Kelly, core constructs, peripheral, superordinate, subordinate, cognitive complexity, Rep Test

concepts.name: Construct Validity
concepts.keywords: validity, construct validity, discriminant validity, measurement

concepts.name: Constructive Alternativism
concepts.keywords: Kelly, constructive alternativism, alternative constructions of reality

concepts.name: Corpus Callosum
concepts.def: Large white matter bundle connecting the two cerebral hemispheres (~250 million axons). Contains genu (frontal fibers), body, and splenium (posterior/visual fibers). Surgical severing (callosotomy) for epilepsy produces split-brain syndrome. Sex differences in size (debated).
concepts.keywords: callosotomy, split-brain, hemispheric communication, genu, splenium, commissures, epilepsy, interhemispheric transfer
concepts.mnemonic: Corpus Callosum = Communication Cable between hemispheres. Cut it = split-brain. Each hand literally doesn't know what the other is doing.

concepts.name: Correlational Research
concepts.keywords: correlation, correlation coefficient, research method, questionnaire, causality

concepts.name: Correlational Research Method
concepts.def: Method examining relationships between variables without manipulation; establishes association not causation; described by correlation coefficient (-1 to +1); common in psychopathology research.
concepts.keywords: correlation, r, correlational method, directionality, third variable, causality, epidemiology, statistical significance

concepts.name: Cortex Layers and Brodmann Areas
concepts.def: Cerebral cortex has 6 layers (I-VI). Layer IV: main input from thalamus; absent in motor cortex (agranular), thickest in sensory cortex. Brodmann parcellated cortex into 52 areas based on cytoarchitecture. Key areas: BA4 (M1), BA3/1/2 (S1), BA17 (V1), BA44/45 (Broca's), BA22 (Wernicke's).
concepts.keywords: Brodmann areas, cortical layers, cytoarchitecture, agranular, granular, BA4, BA17, BA44, layer IV, thalamic input, Brodmann

concepts.name: Cortical Plasticity and Reorganization
concepts.def: Brain's ability to change organization in response to experience or injury. Musicians expand hand representation; blind individuals use visual cortex for touch (cross-modal plasticity); phantom limb from cortical reorganization after amputation. Critical periods for early development.
concepts.keywords: experience-dependent plasticity, cross-modal plasticity, Merzenich, phantom limb, musicians, blind Braille readers, sensory deprivation, critical period

concepts.name: Counterconditioning
concepts.keywords: behavioral therapy, counterconditioning, extinction, classical conditioning, systematic desensitization

concepts.name: D-cycloserine (DCS)
concepts.def: Partial NMDA receptor agonist used as adjunct to exposure therapy; enhances fear extinction learning; studied for anxiety disorders and PTSD.
concepts.keywords: D-cycloserine, DCS, NMDA, fear extinction, exposure augmentation, anxiety, PTSD

concepts.name: DSM-5 (Diagnostic and Statistical Manual, 5th ed.)
concepts.def: APA's primary diagnostic classification system; uses categorical approach; provides criteria, specifiers, and severity ratings for mental disorders; replaced multiaxial system of DSM-IV-TR.
concepts.keywords: DSM-5, APA, diagnostic criteria, categorical, dimensional, multiaxial, reliability, validity, cultural formulation

concepts.name: DSM-5 Major Changes from DSM-IV-TR
concepts.def: APA 2013. Key changes: removed multiaxial system (5 axes); removed bereavement exclusion for MDD; added hoarding disorder and binge eating disorder; merged Asperger into ASD; PTSD moved from anxiety to trauma chapter; added dimensional ratings.
concepts.keywords: reliability, validity, categorical vs dimensional, culture-bound syndromes, NOS, specifiers, comorbidity, bereavement
concepts.mnemonic: DSM-5: REMOVED axes, ADDED hoarding & binge eating, MERGED autism spectrum, MOVED PTSD out of anxiety.

concepts.name: DSM-5 Specifiers (Course, Severity, Features)
concepts.zh: DSM-5指定符（病程、嚴重度、特徵）
concepts.def: Extensions to a DSM-5 diagnosis that further describe course, severity, or distinctive features. Include severity (mild/moderate/severe), course (remission, episodes), and feature specifiers (melancholic, atypical, peripartum, seasonal).
concepts.keywords: specifiers, melancholic, atypical, peripartum, seasonal, mixed features, anxious distress, catatonia, in remission, severity
concepts.mnemonic: Melancholic = mood NON-reactive (worse in AM). Atypical = mood REACTIVE (better with good news) + hypersomnia + weight gain
concepts.page: Section I - Introduction; Section II - throughout

concepts.name: DSM-5 Structure, Organization, and Key Changes
concepts.def: The DSM-5-TR is organized into 3 Sections with a categorical-dimensional approach. Key changes from DSM-IV include abolishing multiaxial system, merging SUD, subsuming ASD subtypes, and removing bereavement exclusion from MDD.
concepts.keywords: DSM-5 structure, Section I II III, multiaxial abolished, categorical dimensional, bereavement exclusion, ASD Asperger, CFI, WHODAS
concepts.mnemonic: DSM-5 = Goodbye axes (no more Axis I-V), Hello dimensions (severity specifiers, WHODAS). Bereavement exclusion REMOVED from MDD.

concepts.name: DTI (Diffusion Tensor Imaging)
concepts.def: MRI-based method tracking water diffusion along white matter tracts to map structural connectivity. Anisotropic diffusion reveals fiber tract organization. Used to study white matter integrity, disconnection syndromes, and language pathways (arcuate fasciculus).
concepts.keywords: white matter tracts, structural connectivity, anisotropic diffusion, fractional anisotropy, tractography, connectome, arcuate fasciculus

concepts.name: Death Anxiety
concepts.keywords: existentialism, terror management theory, mortality salience, death anxiety, Sartre

concepts.name: Decision Making — Neural Basis
concepts.def: Value-based decisions involve OFC/vmPFC (subjective value), ACC (effort cost, conflict), striatum (reward prediction), insula (risk/loss aversion). Model-based (deliberate, PFC) vs. model-free (habitual, BG) systems. Temporal discounting: prefer smaller sooner over larger later rewards.
concepts.keywords: OFC, vmPFC, striatum, insula, loss aversion, temporal discounting, Iowa gambling task, somatic marker hypothesis, Damasio, neuroeconomics

concepts.name: Declarative Memory (Explicit Memory)
concepts.def: Consciously accessible memory including episodic memory (personal events, 'what happened to me') and semantic memory (facts, 'what I know'). Depends on medial temporal lobe especially hippocampus. Impaired in amnesia. Contrasted with nondeclarative (procedural) memory.
concepts.keywords: episodic memory, semantic memory, hippocampus, medial temporal lobe, H.M., amnesia, explicit, conscious access, recall, recognition
concepts.mnemonic: Declarative = you can DECLARE it. 2 types: Episodic (Episodes you lived) + Semantic (S for school facts).

concepts.name: Deep Brain Stimulation (DBS)
concepts.def: Neurosurgical procedure implanting electrodes to deliver continuous electrical stimulation to subcortical targets. Used for Parkinson's disease (STN, GPi), essential tremor, dystonia, OCD. Disrupts pathological oscillations in basal ganglia-thalamic circuits.
concepts.keywords: STN, GPi, Parkinson's, tremor, dystonia, OCD, implanted electrode, neurostimulation, basal ganglia, invasive stimulation

concepts.name: Default Mode Network (DMN)
concepts.def: Brain network most active during rest/mind-wandering and least active during external task performance. Regions: medial PFC, posterior cingulate, precuneus, angular gyrus. Functions: self-referential thought, episodic memory retrieval, social cognition, future planning. Hypoactive in ASD.
concepts.keywords: resting state, self-referential processing, medial PFC, posterior cingulate, precuneus, task-negative, ASD, mind-wandering, Raichle
concepts.mnemonic: Default Mode = Daydreaming Mode. Active when not focused on external task. Think about yourself, others, your future.

concepts.name: Defense Mechanisms
concepts.keywords: repression, projection, denial, rationalization, reaction formation, sublimation, isolation

concepts.name: Delay of Gratification
concepts.keywords: Mischel, marshmallow test, self-control, hot vs cool focus, social-cognitive

concepts.name: Delirium
concepts.def: Acute disturbance of consciousness and cognition; disorientation, attention deficits, perceptual disturbances; reversible; common in elderly; multiple etiologies.
concepts.keywords: delirium, acute confusion, cognitive disturbance, reversible, elderly, delirium tremens, medical causes

concepts.name: Dementia (Major Neurocognitive Disorder)
concepts.def: Progressive decline in memory and cognitive functions; includes Alzheimer's, vascular, frontotemporal, Lewy body types; DSM-5 term: major neurocognitive disorder.
concepts.keywords: dementia, neurocognitive disorder, Alzheimer's, vascular, frontotemporal, Lewy body, progressive, DSM-5

concepts.name: Denial (Defense Mechanism)
concepts.keywords: defense mechanism, denial, Freud, incongruence, Rogers

concepts.name: Depersonalization/Derealization Disorder
concepts.def: Persistent feelings of detachment from one's mental processes or body (depersonalization) or sense of unreality of surroundings (derealization), without loss of reality testing.
concepts.keywords: depersonalization, derealization, dissociative, detachment, reality testing intact, DSM-5

concepts.name: Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT)
concepts.def: Developed by Linehan for BPD. Combines CBT with mindfulness and dialectics (acceptance + change). Four skill modules: mindfulness, distress tolerance, emotion regulation, interpersonal effectiveness.
concepts.keywords: Linehan, BPD, dialectics, mindfulness, biosocial theory, validation, skills training, acceptance
concepts.mnemonic: DBT = Dialectics (BOTH acceptance AND change). MDIE modules: Mindfulness, Distress tolerance, Interpersonal, Emotion regulation.

concepts.name: Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT)
concepts.def: Linehan's treatment for BPD; integrates CBT with acceptance strategies and mindfulness; reduces self-harm and suicidality; components: individual therapy, skills group, phone coaching, consultation.
concepts.keywords: DBT, dialectical, Linehan, BPD, borderline, mindfulness, acceptance, distress tolerance, emotional regulation, suicide

concepts.name: Diathesis-Stress Model
concepts.def: Psychological disorders result from an interaction between a predisposition (diathesis - genetic, biological, or psychological vulnerability) and environmental stressors. Neither alone is sufficient.
concepts.keywords: vulnerability, predisposition, stress, gene-environment interaction, epigenetics, threshold
concepts.mnemonic: DIATHESIS = underlying vulnerability DORMANT until stress triggers it. Seed + Soil analogy.

concepts.name: Diathesis-Stress Model
concepts.def: Integrative paradigm: disorders result from interaction of pre-existing vulnerability (diathesis) and environmental stress; neither alone is sufficient; applies to all major disorders.
concepts.keywords: diathesis, stress, vulnerability, gene-environment interaction, predisposition, trigger, integrative model

concepts.name: Dissociative Amnesia
concepts.def: Inability to recall important autobiographical information, usually traumatic; can include dissociative fugue (unexpected travel, identity confusion); not due to substance or neurological condition.
concepts.keywords: dissociative amnesia, fugue, trauma, memory, retrograde, psychogenic, abuse, recovered memories

concepts.name: Dissociative Identity Disorder (DID)
concepts.def: Presence of two or more distinct personality states; linked to childhood trauma; controversial; etiology debated (posttraumatic vs. sociocognitive models); treatment involves integration.
concepts.keywords: DID, multiple personality, dissociative identity, trauma, posttraumatic model, sociocognitive model, Bianchi, Sybil

concepts.name: Dopamine
concepts.keywords: dopamine, neurotransmitter, reward, temperament, brain

concepts.name: Dopamine Hypothesis (Schizophrenia)
concepts.def: Proposes that excess dopaminergic activity in mesolimbic pathway underlies positive symptoms of schizophrenia; supported by antipsychotic D2 blockade; revised to include glutamate and D1 deficits in prefrontal cortex.
concepts.keywords: dopamine hypothesis, schizophrenia, D2 receptor, mesolimbic, antipsychotic, glutamate, prefrontal, positive symptoms

concepts.name: Dopamine Hypothesis of Schizophrenia
concepts.def: Excess dopamine activity (especially in mesolimbic pathway) underlies positive symptoms of schizophrenia. Evidence: antipsychotics block D2 receptors; amphetamines (which increase DA) produce psychosis.
concepts.keywords: D2 receptors, mesolimbic, mesocortical, chlorpromazine, first-generation antipsychotics, amphetamine psychosis
concepts.mnemonic: Too much DA = Too many Delusions and Auditory hallucinations. Block DA = Block symptoms.

concepts.name: Dopamine Pathways
concepts.def: Four main dopamine pathways: (1) Mesolimbic: VTA to nucleus accumbens — reward, addiction; (2) Mesocortical: VTA to PFC — working memory, cognition; (3) Nigrostriatal: SN to striatum — motor control (Parkinson's); (4) Tuberoinfundibular: hypothalamus — hormone regulation.
concepts.keywords: mesolimbic, mesocortical, nigrostriatal, VTA, substantia nigra, reward, Parkinson's, prediction error, wanting vs liking
concepts.mnemonic: 4 DA paths: MMNT (Mesolimbic-reward, Mesocortical-cognition, Nigrostriatal-movement, Tuberoinfundibular-hormones)

concepts.name: Dorsal Attention Network (DAN) Key Regions
concepts.def: Key nodes: Frontal Eye Fields (FEF, BA8) and Intraparietal Sulcus (IPS). Controls voluntary spatial attention via top-down biasing of visual cortex. FEF: plans eye movements, also covertly shifts attention. IPS: maintains priority maps, integrates spatial information. Bilateral but asymmetric.
concepts.keywords: FEF, IPS, BA8, intraparietal sulcus, frontal eye fields, top-down attention, spatial priority map, covert attention, eye movements

concepts.name: Dorsal and Ventral Visual Streams
concepts.def: Two processing pathways from V1: (1) Ventral stream (V1 to IT cortex) = 'what' pathway for object recognition and identification; (2) Dorsal stream (V1 to parietal cortex) = 'where/how' pathway for spatial location and action guidance. Case D.F.: ventral damage = can't recognize but can grasp.
concepts.keywords: what pathway, where pathway, ventral occipitotemporal, dorsal occipitoparietal, IT cortex, parietal cortex, D.F., Goodale, Milner, visual agnosia
concepts.mnemonic: Ventral = WHAT is it? (goes down to temporal lobe). Dorsal = WHERE is it? (goes up to parietal). D.F. had 'what' damage: no object ID but perfect grasping.

concepts.name: Dorsal vs. Ventral Visual Streams
concepts.def: Ungerleider and Mishkin (1982): Ventral stream (occipitotemporal) = WHAT pathway for object identification. Dorsal stream (occipitoparietal) = WHERE/HOW pathway for spatial location and action. Goodale and Milner refined to: ventral = perception for recognition, dorsal = perception for action.
concepts.keywords: Ungerleider, Mishkin, Goodale, Milner, visual agnosia, optic ataxia, temporal lobe, parietal lobe, what pathway, where pathway, action, recognition
concepts.mnemonic: WHAT is in the VENTRAL (bottom) stream. WHERE/HOW is in the DORSAL (top) stream. V for Ventral-Vision-What. D for Dorsal-Direction-Where.

concepts.name: Double-Blind Procedure
concepts.def: Experimental control where both participants and assessors are unaware of condition assignment; minimizes expectancy effects and demand characteristics in clinical trials.
concepts.keywords: double-blind, expectancy, placebo, randomized controlled trial, internal validity, drug trial

concepts.name: Dual-Process Theory (System 1 vs System 2)
concepts.def: Two cognitive systems: System 1 (fast, automatic, unconscious) and System 2 (slow, deliberate, conscious, high WM load). Kahneman 2011.
concepts.keywords: System 1, System 2, fast thinking, slow thinking, heuristics, cognitive bias, automatic processing, confirmation bias
concepts.mnemonic: System 1 = GUT (Gut-fast, Unconscious, Typical errors); System 2 = HARD (High effort, Analytical, Requires deliberation)

concepts.name: Dynamic Traits
concepts.keywords: Cattell, dynamic traits, motivation, erg, sentiment

concepts.name: EEG (Electroencephalography)
concepts.def: Records electrical activity from scalp electrodes. Excellent temporal resolution (milliseconds), poor spatial resolution. Measures postsynaptic potentials summed across many neurons. Used for epilepsy diagnosis, sleep staging, ERPs.
concepts.keywords: temporal resolution, spatial resolution, postsynaptic potentials, ERPs, alpha waves, sleep stages, epilepsy, P300, N400

concepts.name: Effortful Control
concepts.keywords: temperament, effortful control, self-regulation, inhibition, conscience

concepts.name: Ego
concepts.keywords: Freud, ego, reality principle, id, superego, defense mechanisms

concepts.name: Electroconvulsive Therapy (ECT)
concepts.def: Biological treatment using brief electrical brain stimulation under anesthesia; highly effective for severe/treatment-resistant depression; temporary memory side effects; unilateral preferred.
concepts.keywords: ECT, electroconvulsive, depression, severe, treatment-resistant, memory, bilateral, unilateral, Cerletti, Bini

concepts.name: Ellis, Albert
concepts.keywords: rational-emotive therapy, RET, REBT, irrational beliefs, cognitive therapy

concepts.name: Emotion Theories (James-Lange, Cannon-Bard, Appraisal)
concepts.def: James-Lange: bodily changes precede and CAUSE emotion (you see bear -> run -> FEEL fear). Cannon-Bard: body and emotion occur simultaneously (thalamus sends signal to both cortex and body). Appraisal theory: cognitive evaluation of event determines emotional response. LeDoux: fast (low road) and slow (high road) pathways.
concepts.keywords: James-Lange, Cannon-Bard, appraisal theory, LeDoux, high road, low road, amygdala, thalamus, bodily state, cognitive appraisal
concepts.mnemonic: James-Lange: Body -> Emotion (run -> fear). Cannon-Bard: Both simultaneously. Appraisal: Think -> Emotion. LeDoux: Fast thalamic (low road) + slow cortical (high road).
concepts.theorists: James, Lange, Cannon, Bard, Lazarus, LeDoux, Schachter

concepts.name: Empathy — Neural Substrates
concepts.def: Sharing another's emotional/pain state. Experience-sharing theory: mirror neuron system, insula, and ACC activate as if personally experiencing the emotion. Tania Singer studies: watching partner in pain activates pain matrix (insula/ACC but NOT sensory or motor cortex). Empathy modulated by group membership.
concepts.keywords: mirror neurons, insula, ACC, pain empathy, Singer, simulation theory, theory of mind, affective empathy, in-group out-group

concepts.name: Epigenetics
concepts.def: Study of heritable changes in gene expression not involving DNA sequence changes; environmental experiences (stress, parenting) modify gene expression; relevant to psychopathology etiology.
concepts.keywords: epigenetics, gene expression, methylation, licking and grooming, arched-back nursing, environment, heritable changes

concepts.name: Erikson, Erik
concepts.keywords: psychosocial stages, identity, ego development, lifespan

concepts.name: Event-Related Potentials (ERPs)
concepts.def: EEG signals time-locked to stimulus events, averaged to reveal consistent neural responses. Key components: P300 (attention/target detection), N400 (semantic mismatch), P600 (syntactic violation). Excellent temporal resolution.
concepts.keywords: P300, N400, P600, LAN, time-locked, averaging, semantic processing, syntactic processing, attention, EEG
concepts.mnemonic: ERPs: N400 = No match (semantic), P600 = Problem with grammar (syntactic). N=negative, P=positive deflection.

concepts.name: Evolutionary Psychology
concepts.keywords: evolution, mate preferences, sex differences, jealousy, domain specificity, parental investment

concepts.name: Existentialism
concepts.keywords: Sartre, existentialism, free will, mortality salience, terror management, death anxiety

concepts.name: Expectancies (Social-Cognitive Theory)
concepts.keywords: expectancies, outcome expectation, self-efficacy, social-cognitive, anchoring

concepts.name: Experimental Research
concepts.keywords: experiment, random assignment, causality, laboratory, internal validity

concepts.name: Exposure Therapy
concepts.def: Systematic confrontation with feared stimuli to extinguish conditioned fear responses; delivered in vivo or imaginal; cornerstone of anxiety disorder treatments; includes flooding and gradual exposure.
concepts.keywords: exposure therapy, in vivo, imaginal, flooding, systematic desensitization, fear extinction, anxiety, phobia, PTSD

concepts.name: Expressed Emotion (EE)
concepts.def: High levels of criticism, hostility, and emotional over-involvement from family members toward a patient; predicts relapse in schizophrenia and depression; targeted in family therapy.
concepts.keywords: expressed emotion, EE, family, criticism, hostility, over-involvement, schizophrenia relapse, family therapy

concepts.name: Eysenck's Personality Theory
concepts.keywords: superfactors, extraversion, neuroticism, psychoticism, biological basis, lemon drop test

concepts.name: Eysenck, Hans J.
concepts.keywords: PEN model, extraversion, neuroticism, psychoticism, biological traits

concepts.name: Factor Analysis
concepts.keywords: factor analysis, trait measurement, statistical method, covariation, subjective decisions

concepts.name: Fear Circuit
concepts.def: Neural network including amygdala, hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, and thalamus; processes threat and fear conditioning; disrupted in anxiety disorders and PTSD.
concepts.keywords: fear circuit, amygdala, hippocampus, prefrontal cortex, threat, anxiety, PTSD, fear conditioning, LeDoux

concepts.name: Fear Conditioning
concepts.def: Classical conditioning paradigm where neutral CS (tone) is paired with aversive UCS (shock) to produce conditioned fear CR. Critically depends on the amygdala (lateral nucleus for acquisition, central nucleus for expression). High road (cortex) and low road (thalamus-amygdala) pathways.
concepts.keywords: CS, UCS, CR, amygdala, lateral nucleus, central nucleus, high road, low road, LeDoux, extinction, conditioned fear
concepts.mnemonic: Fear Conditioning: Tone + Shock = Fear. AMYGDALA does it. Low road = fast and dirty. High road = slow and accurate.

concepts.name: Feeding and Eating Disorders (AN, BN, BED)
concepts.zh: 飲食障礙（厘食症、暴食症、暴食症）
concepts.def: Disorders characterized by disturbed eating behaviors: Anorexia Nervosa (low weight, fear of weight gain, body distortion), Bulimia Nervosa (binge-purge), Binge-Eating Disorder (binge without compensating).
concepts.keywords: anorexia nervosa, bulimia nervosa, binge eating disorder, restricting type, purging, Russell's sign, amenorrhea removed, highest mortality
concepts.mnemonic: AN = low weight + fear weight gain + body distortion. BN = binge + purge at normal weight. BED = binge without purge
concepts.page: Section II - Feeding and Eating Disorders

concepts.name: Five-Factor Model of Personality
concepts.keywords: Big Five, OCEAN, NEO-PI-R, openness, conscientiousness, extraversion, agreeableness, neuroticism

concepts.name: Fixed-Role Therapy
concepts.keywords: Kelly, fixed-role therapy, personal construct, behavior change, enactment

concepts.name: Free Association
concepts.keywords: Freud, free association, psychoanalysis, unconscious, technique

concepts.name: Freud, Sigmund
concepts.keywords: psychoanalysis, unconscious, id, ego, superego, psychosexual stages, defense mechanisms

concepts.name: Functional Analysis of Behavior
concepts.keywords: Skinner, functional analysis, ABC assessment, behaviorism, antecedent-behavior-consequence

concepts.name: Functional Neurological Disorder (Conversion Disorder)
concepts.def: Neurological symptoms (paralysis, blindness, seizures) inconsistent with known neurological conditions; classified under somatic symptom disorders; psychological stress often precipitates.
concepts.keywords: conversion disorder, functional neurological, motor symptoms, sensory symptoms, hysteria, glove anesthesia, blindsight, stress

concepts.name: Fundamental Lexical Hypothesis
concepts.keywords: Big Five, lexical hypothesis, trait terms, natural language, trait words

concepts.name: Fusiform Face Area (FFA)
concepts.def: Region in fusiform gyrus (right hemisphere dominant) selectively responding to faces. Identified by Kanwisher et al. using fMRI. Responds more to faces than objects. Damage causes prosopagnosia. Expertise hypothesis: responds to any stimulus in domain of expertise (e.g., dog experts, car experts).
concepts.keywords: Kanwisher, fusiform gyrus, face recognition, prosopagnosia, category specificity, expertise hypothesis, right hemisphere
concepts.mnemonic: FFA = Face-Fixated Area. Lights up for faces. Controversy: face-specific or expertise-specific?

concepts.name: Fusiform Face Area (FFA) & Prosopagnosia
concepts.def: FFA (fusiform gyrus, ventral temporal cortex): responds selectively to faces more than other objects. Kanwisher proposed face-specific module. Prosopagnosia: inability to recognize familiar faces after FFA/right occipitotemporal damage. Can be acquired (brain damage) or developmental (congenital). Preserved non-face object recognition.
concepts.keywords: Kanwisher, fusiform face area, prosopagnosia, face-specific, temporal lobe, holistic processing, developmental prosopagnosia, visual agnosia, right hemisphere
concepts.mnemonic: FFA = Face Factory Area. Always running face-recognition programs. Damage = Face blindness (prosopagnosia). Right hemisphere is dominant for face processing.

concepts.name: Gene-Environment Interaction
concepts.def: Genetic predispositions moderate the effect of environmental experiences on psychopathology; e.g., 5-HTT gene and stressful life events predicting depression (Caspi et al.).
concepts.keywords: gene-environment interaction, GxE, serotonin transporter, 5-HTT, stress, Caspi, diathesis, epigenetics, substance use

concepts.name: Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD)
concepts.def: Excessive, uncontrollable worry about multiple areas for at least 6 months; accompanied by physical symptoms; etiology involves intolerance of uncertainty, cognitive avoidance; treated with CBT, buspirone, SSRIs.
concepts.keywords: GAD, worry, uncontrollable, 6 months, buspirone, CBT, intolerance of uncertainty, cognitive avoidance, tension

concepts.name: Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD)
concepts.def: Excessive, difficult-to-control anxiety and worry about multiple events/activities, >=6 months, with >=3 associated physical symptoms, causing significant distress.
concepts.keywords: GAD, generalized anxiety, 6 months, uncontrollable worry, restlessness, muscle tension, sleep, free-floating
concepts.mnemonic: 3 for 6: 3 symptoms (1 in children), 6 months. WRRIMS: Worry, Restlessness, Rest (fatigue), Irritability, Muscle tension, Sleep

concepts.name: Glial Cells
concepts.def: Non-neuronal brain cells (90% of brain cells). Types: Astrocytes (support, BBB, neurotransmitter clearance); Oligodendrocytes (myelin in CNS); Schwann cells (myelin in PNS); Microglia (immune, phagocytosis). Astrocytes modulate synaptic transmission. Glial tumors = gliomas/astrocytomas.
concepts.keywords: astrocyte, oligodendrocyte, Schwann cell, microglia, myelin, BBB, neurotransmitter clearance, glioma, support cells

concepts.name: Goals (Social-Cognitive Theory)
concepts.keywords: goals, self-regulation, social-cognitive, learning vs performance goals, motivation

concepts.name: Guided Mastery
concepts.keywords: Bandura, guided mastery, self-efficacy, anxiety treatment, observational learning

concepts.name: H.M. (Patient Henry Molaison)
concepts.def: The most famous case study in memory research. After bilateral medial temporal lobe removal (1953, surgeon Scoville), H.M. had profound anterograde amnesia but intact short-term memory, procedural memory, and priming. Proved hippocampus is critical for declarative memory consolidation.
concepts.keywords: anterograde amnesia, declarative memory, procedural memory, hippocampus, medial temporal lobe, Scoville, Milner, double dissociation, case study
concepts.mnemonic: HM = He Memorized nothing new after surgery. But he could still learn motor skills (procedural). Short-term memory INTACT. Long-term declarative GONE.

concepts.name: H.M. (Patient Henry Molaison) Case
concepts.def: Landmark case: bilateral medial temporal lobectomy (including hippocampus) for epilepsy in 1953. Resulted in: severe anterograde amnesia, mild retrograde amnesia, spared short-term memory, spared procedural learning (mirror tracing), spared language and perception. Established hippocampus = essential for declarative memory.
concepts.keywords: bilateral hippocampectomy, anterograde amnesia, declarative memory, procedural memory spared, Scoville, Milner, medial temporal lobe
concepts.mnemonic: H.M. = Hippocampus Missing. Can't make new declarative memories but CAN learn motor skills. Hippocampus = essential for new explicit memories.

concepts.name: HPA Axis & Stress Response
concepts.def: Hypothalamic-Pituitary-Adrenal axis: stress activates hypothalamus -> CRF -> pituitary -> ACTH -> adrenal cortex -> cortisol. Chronic activation damages hippocampus, implicated in MDD and PTSD.
concepts.keywords: cortisol, CRF, ACTH, dexamethasone suppression test, hippocampus damage, depression, PTSD, allostatic load
concepts.mnemonic: HPA Highway: Hypothalamus -> Pituitary -> Adrenal -> Cortisol. Think: STRESS climbs the HPA ladder.

concepts.name: HPA Axis (Hypothalamic-Pituitary-Adrenal Axis)
concepts.def: Neuroendocrine stress response system; CRF triggers ACTH which releases cortisol; chronically elevated in depression; involved in PTSD, schizophrenia, and anxiety disorders.
concepts.keywords: HPA axis, cortisol, CRF, ACTH, stress response, depression, PTSD, hypothalamus, pituitary, adrenal, dexamethasone suppression

concepts.name: Hebbian Learning
concepts.def: Hebb's postulate: neurons that fire together wire together. Simultaneous pre- and postsynaptic activity strengthens the synapse. Foundational principle underlying LTP and associative learning. Anti-Hebbian: if only pre fires without post, synapse weakens (LTD).
concepts.keywords: fire together wire together, LTP, associative learning, synaptic plasticity, NMDA receptor, coincidence detection
concepts.mnemonic: Hebb's rule: cells that FIRE together WIRE together. Think of Hebb as the original #relationship goals of neurons.

concepts.name: Hemispheric Specialization and Lateralization
concepts.def: Left hemisphere: language (95% right-handers), sequential/analytic processing, positive affect. Right hemisphere: visuospatial processing, holistic/gestalt processing, prosody, emotion recognition. Assessed by Wada test (amobarbital injection). Planum temporale asymmetry underlies language lateralization.
concepts.keywords: Wada test, left hemisphere language, right visuospatial, split-brain, dichotic listening, planum temporale, handedness, amobarbital
concepts.mnemonic: Left = Language and Logic. Right = Recognition and visuoSpatial. Wada test: inject amobarbital to sleep one hemisphere, test the other.

concepts.name: Heritability Coefficient

concepts.name: Heuristics & Cognitive Biases (5 Types)
concepts.def: Mental shortcuts that lead to systematic errors: Availability, Representativeness, Base Rate Neglect, Conjunction Fallacy, Law of Small Numbers.
concepts.keywords: availability heuristic, representativeness heuristic, base rate neglect, conjunction fallacy, law of small numbers, Linda problem, cognitive bias
concepts.mnemonic: ARBCL = Availability, Representativeness, Base rate, Conjunction, Law of small numbers

concepts.name: Hippocampus
concepts.def: Medial temporal lobe structure critical for declarative memory encoding and consolidation. H.M. case: bilateral hippocampectomy produced severe anterograde amnesia but spared procedural memory. Contains place cells. LTP occurs here via Schaffer collateral-CA1 pathway.
concepts.keywords: H.M., anterograde amnesia, declarative memory, place cells, CA1, CA3, dentate gyrus, LTP, spatial memory, consolidation
concepts.mnemonic: Hippocampus = HIPPO in your Memory Palace. Without it: can't make new declarative memories. H.M. couldn't remember yesterday.

concepts.name: Hippocampus
concepts.def: Limbic structure critical for memory consolidation; decreased volume in PTSD, depression, and schizophrenia; affected in Alzheimer's disease; involved in fear extinction.
concepts.keywords: hippocampus, memory, PTSD volume reduction, Alzheimer's, schizophrenia, fear extinction, stress, cortisol

concepts.name: Hoarding Disorder
concepts.def: Persistent difficulty discarding possessions; significant distress/impairment; classified as OCD-related disorder in DSM-5; distinct from OCD; cognitive-behavioral treatment.
concepts.keywords: hoarding disorder, OCD-related, DSM-5, clutter, distress, cognitive-behavioral, excessive acquisition

concepts.name: Huntington's Disease
concepts.def: Autosomal dominant neurodegenerative disorder. CAG repeat expansion in HTT gene on chromosome 4 -> huntingtin protein aggregation -> progressive striatal (caudate/putamen) neuron loss. Symptoms: chorea, dementia, psychiatric symptoms. Impairs procedural memory. No cure, genetic test available.
concepts.keywords: CAG repeat, HTT gene, striatum, caudate, chorea, autosomal dominant, procedural learning, genetic test, anticipation
concepts.mnemonic: Huntington's = Hunt your own neurons. CHOREA = uncontrolled dance movements. CAG repeats grow with generations (anticipation).

concepts.name: Insula (Insular Cortex)
concepts.def: Hidden cortex deep in lateral sulcus. Functions: interoception (body state awareness), disgust, pain, social emotions, taste (secondary gustatory cortex), empathy. Anterior insula: subjective feeling states. Damasio: insula maps body states for somatic markers. Part of salience network.
concepts.keywords: interoception, disgust, pain, empathy, taste, salience network, body state, Damasio, anterior insula, somatic marker
concepts.mnemonic: Insula = INside feelings. Tastes bad? Insula fires. In pain? Insula fires. Empathy? Insula fires. The body's emotional thermometer.

concepts.name: Intellectual Developmental Disorder (IDD)
concepts.def: Significant deficits in intellectual functioning (IQ < 70) and adaptive behavior; onset during developmental period; etiology includes chromosomal, genetic, and environmental causes.
concepts.keywords: IDD, intellectual disability, IQ below 70, adaptive behavior, Down syndrome, Fragile X, DSM-5, ABA treatment

concepts.name: Intellectual Developmental Disorder (Intellectual Disability)
concepts.zh: 智力發展障礙（智能障礙）
concepts.def: A neurodevelopmental disorder with onset in the developmental period, characterized by deficits in both intellectual functions and adaptive behavior in conceptual, social, and practical domains.
concepts.keywords: IDD, intellectual disability, adaptive functioning, IQ less than 70, Rosa's Law, Down syndrome, Flynn effect
concepts.mnemonic: IDD = IQ + ADL + Age (IQ<=70, Deficits in Adaptive functioning, Developmental onset)

concepts.name: Interpersonal Psychotherapy (IPT)
concepts.def: Time-limited therapy targeting interpersonal problems (grief, role disputes, role transitions, social deficits) linked to depression onset; empirically validated for depression, bulimia, BED.
concepts.keywords: IPT, interpersonal, depression, grief, role dispute, role transition, Klerman, Weissman, bulimia, BED

concepts.name: Language Comprehension — Spoken and Written
concepts.def: Spoken: acoustic -> phonemes (STG) -> words (STG/MTG) -> syntax (IFG/STG) -> semantics (widespread temporal-frontal). Written: orthography -> visual word form area (left occipitotemporal) -> phonological and semantic processing. Lexical access involves mental lexicon in left temporal-frontal networks.
concepts.keywords: visual word form area, mental lexicon, phonological processing, semantic processing, STG, IFG, arcuate fasciculus, reading, dyslexia, alexia

concepts.name: Language Development Theories (Nativist, Social Interaction, Interactionist)
concepts.def: Three theories: Nativist (LAD, Chomsky), Social Interaction (ZPD, IDS, Tomasello), Interactionist (combines both). Fast-mapping and syntactic bootstrapping as key mechanisms.
concepts.keywords: LAD, universal grammar, infant-directed speech, fast-mapping, syntactic bootstrapping, ZPD, scaffolding, social interaction theory, nativist, interactionist, perceptual narrowing
concepts.mnemonic: NSI = Nativist (Nature), Social Interaction (Nurture), Interactionist (Nature+Nurture together)

concepts.name: Language Disorder
concepts.def: Persistent difficulties in language acquisition and use across modalities due to deficits in comprehension or production, resulting in functional limitations.
concepts.keywords: language disorder, expressive, receptive, discourse, vocabulary, grammar, communication, developmental

concepts.def: Specific difficulties in reading (dyslexia), math (dyscalculia), or written expression; neurobiological etiology; phonological processing deficits in dyslexia; treated with targeted educational intervention.
concepts.keywords: dyslexia, dyscalculia, learning disability, phonological, specific learning disorder, DSM-5, fast ForWord, reading

concepts.name: Limbic System
concepts.def: Circuit proposed by Papez as the emotional brain. Components: hippocampus, amygdala, hypothalamus, anterior thalamus, cingulate gyrus, mammillary bodies (Papez circuit). Now considered an oversimplification. MacLean expanded to triune brain (reptilian, paleomammalian, neomammalian). Emotion involves broader distributed networks.
concepts.keywords: Papez circuit, amygdala, hippocampus, cingulate gyrus, hypothalamus, MacLean, triune brain, emotional brain, mammillary bodies

concepts.name: Lithium
concepts.def: Mood stabilizer; first-line treatment for bipolar disorder; prophylactic against manic and depressive episodes; narrow therapeutic window; reduces suicidal behavior.
concepts.keywords: lithium, mood stabilizer, bipolar disorder, manic episode, narrow window, prophylactic, anti-suicide, monitoring

concepts.name: Long-Term Potentiation (LTP)
concepts.def: The cellular mechanism of learning and memory. Repeated synaptic stimulation produces a lasting increase in synaptic strength. Requires NMDA receptor activation, calcium influx, and AMPA receptor insertion. Discovered in hippocampus by Bliss and Lomo (1973).
concepts.keywords: Bliss, Lomo, NMDA receptors, AMPA receptors, calcium, hippocampus, Hebb's law, coincidence detector, synapse, memory consolidation
concepts.mnemonic: LTP = Learning Turns Persistent. Hebb's rule: neurons that fire together wire together. NMDA is the coincidence detector (needs pre AND post activation).

concepts.name: Long-Term Potentiation (LTP)
concepts.def: Activity-dependent strengthening of synapses. Requires NMDA receptor activation (coincidence detector: pre AND post must fire). Results in AMPA receptor insertion and dendritic spine growth. Cellular basis of memory.
concepts.keywords: NMDA receptor, AMPA receptor, Hebbian learning, coincidence detector, synaptic plasticity, hippocampus, LTP, memory, engram
concepts.mnemonic: LTP = Learning Through Pairing. NMDA opens only if BOTH pre AND post active (Hebb rule: fire together wire together).

concepts.name: M'Naghten & ALI Insanity Defense Standards
concepts.def: M'Naghten (1843): Not guilty if did not know nature of act OR did not know it was wrong. ALI/Model Penal Code: Lacked substantial capacity to appreciate criminality OR conform conduct to law. IDRA 1984 narrowed standard post-Hinckley.
concepts.keywords: NGRI, guilty but mentally ill, competency to stand trial, insanity defense reform act, Hinckley, ALI, M'Naghten
concepts.mnemonic: M'Naghten = Must Not Know (what you did or that it was wrong). ALI = Appreciate + conform (broader, behaviorally).

concepts.name: M'Naghten Rule
concepts.def: Oldest insanity standard: defendant did not know nature of act or that it was wrong due to mental disease; most common standard in U.S. states; narrower than ALI.
concepts.keywords: M'Naghten, insanity defense, did not know, right from wrong, criminal, mental disease, Daniel M'Naghten, NGRI

concepts.name: MEG (Magnetoencephalography)
concepts.def: Measures magnetic fields produced by electrical currents in neurons using SQUID sensors. Millisecond temporal resolution like EEG, but slightly better spatial resolution. Measures tangential (not radial) sources. Good for auditory and somatosensory cortex studies. Very expensive, requires magnetically shielded room.
concepts.keywords: SQUID, temporal resolution, magnetic field, EEG comparison, spatial resolution, auditory cortex, somatosensory, shielded room

concepts.name: MMPI-2 (Minnesota Multiphasic Personality Inventory)
concepts.def: Most widely used self-report personality inventory (567 T/F items). 10 clinical scales + 3 validity scales (L lie, F infrequency, K defensiveness). Empirically keyed. Used for psychopathology screening and forensic assessment.
concepts.keywords: validity scales, L F K scales, clinical scales, T-scores, empirically keyed, profile, elevated scales, fake good fake bad
concepts.mnemonic: MMPI validity trinity: L (Lie), F (Faking bad), K (defensive). Clinical scales: Hs D Hy Pd Mf Pa Pt Sc Ma Si.

concepts.name: Major Depressive Disorder (MDD)
concepts.def: DSM-5: 5+ symptoms for 2+ weeks, must include depressed mood OR anhedonia. Other symptoms: weight change, sleep disturbance, psychomotor changes, fatigue, worthlessness, poor concentration, suicidal ideation.
concepts.keywords: SIG E CAPS, anhedonia, bereavement exclusion removed, specifiers, melancholic, psychotic, seasonal, recurrent
concepts.mnemonic: SIG E CAPS: Sleep, Interest/anhedonia, Guilt, Energy, Concentration, Appetite, Psychomotor, Suicidal ideation. Need 5 of 9 for 2+ weeks.

concepts.name: Major Depressive Disorder (MDD)
concepts.def: Two or more weeks of depressed mood/anhedonia plus 5+ symptoms; high prevalence; multiple etiological factors; treated with CBT, IPT, antidepressants, or combination.
concepts.keywords: MDD, major depression, SIG E CAPS, anhedonia, hopelessness, cognitive triad, serotonin, CBT, IPT, antidepressants

concepts.name: Major Depressive Disorder (MDD)
concepts.def: A depressive disorder characterized by >=5 depressive symptoms during a 2-week period (including depressed mood or anhedonia), causing significant functional impairment.
concepts.keywords: MDD, major depression, SIG E CAPS, anhedonia, 5 of 9 symptoms, 2 weeks, peripartum, melancholic, atypical, suicidality
concepts.mnemonic: SIG E CAPS: Sleep, Interest, Guilt, Energy, Concentration, Appetite, Psychomotor, Suicidality

concepts.name: Memory Consolidation
concepts.def: Process by which newly encoded memories become stabilized and stored long-term. Two stages: (1) cellular/synaptic consolidation (hours, protein synthesis, LTP); (2) systems consolidation (weeks to years, hippocampus to cortex). Sleep facilitates consolidation (slow-wave and REM phases).
concepts.keywords: systems consolidation, synaptic consolidation, hippocampus to cortex, standard consolidation theory, multiple trace theory, sleep, protein synthesis

concepts.name: Memory Systems Taxonomy (Declarative vs Nondeclarative)
concepts.def: Declarative (explicit): Episodic + Semantic. Nondeclarative (implicit): Procedural, Priming, Conditioning. Hippocampus for consolidation not storage.
concepts.keywords: declarative, episodic, semantic, nondeclarative, procedural, priming, hippocampus, encoding, retrieval, working memory, levels of processing
concepts.mnemonic: DESk = Declarative (Episodic + Semantic). NPC = Nondeclarative (Procedural + Conditioning)
concepts.theorists: Tulving, Squire, Baddeley, Craik & Lockhart, Atkinson & Shiffrin

concepts.name: Meta-Analysis
concepts.def: Statistical technique combining results from multiple studies to estimate overall effect size; increases statistical power; used to evaluate treatment efficacy across trials.
concepts.keywords: meta-analysis, effect size, systematic review, Smith & Glass, treatment efficacy, heterogeneity, outcome research

concepts.name: Mindfulness-Based Cognitive Therapy (MBCT)
concepts.def: Integrates CBT with mindfulness meditation; primarily used to prevent depressive relapse in patients with 3+ previous episodes; based on Segal, Williams, Teasdale.
concepts.keywords: MBCT, mindfulness, cognitive therapy, relapse prevention, depression, Segal, Williams, Teasdale, acceptance
concepts.theorists: Zindel Segal, Mark Williams, John Teasdale

concepts.name: Minnesota Multiphasic Personality Inventory (MMPI-2)
concepts.def: Most widely used self-report personality inventory; 567 true/false items; 10 clinical scales plus validity scales; used for diagnosis, treatment planning, forensic assessment.
concepts.keywords: MMPI, MMPI-2, personality inventory, validity scales, clinical scales, self-report, L F K scales, 567 items, Hathaway

concepts.name: Mirror Neuron System
concepts.def: Neurons in premotor cortex (F5 in monkeys, BA 44 in humans) that fire both when an animal performs an action AND when it observes another performing the same action. Proposed role in imitation, empathy, language evolution, and theory of mind. Discovered in macaques by Rizzolatti.
concepts.keywords: premotor cortex, F5, action observation, imitation, empathy, language evolution, autism, Rizzolatti, motor resonance
concepts.mnemonic: Mirror neurons = Neural empathy. They MIRROR what they see. I watch you reach -> my motor neurons respond AS IF I was reaching.

concepts.name: Mirror Neurons
concepts.def: Discovered by Rizzolatti in macaque premotor cortex (F5). Neurons fire both when the animal performs an action AND when it observes the same action performed by another. Proposed role in action understanding, imitation, theory of mind, empathy, and language evolution. Highly debated in humans (evidence indirect via fMRI).
concepts.keywords: Rizzolatti, F5, premotor cortex, action understanding, imitation, empathy, theory of mind, language evolution, simulation theory, ASD mirror neuron deficit
concepts.mnemonic: Mirror neurons MIRROR what you see. Fire when you DO it and when you WATCH it. Your brain rehearses others actions as if they were your own.

concepts.name: Mischel, Walter
concepts.keywords: CAPS, delay of gratification, social-cognitive, person-situation controversy, marshmallow test

concepts.name: N400 and P600 (Language ERPs)
concepts.def: N400: negative ERP component ~400ms, elicited by semantically unexpected words. Index of semantic integration difficulty. P600: positive ERP ~600ms, elicited by grammatical violations. Index of syntactic reanalysis. Key tools for studying real-time language comprehension.
concepts.keywords: N400, P600, semantic mismatch, syntactic violation, Kutas, ERP, LAN, language comprehension, grammar
concepts.mnemonic: N400 = No semantic sense at 400ms. P600 = Problem with grammar at 600ms. N=negative, P=positive.
