concepts.keywords: NEO-PI-R, Big Five, Costa, McCrae, personality assessment, facets, six facets

concepts.name: NMDA Receptor
concepts.def: Glutamate receptor that acts as a coincidence detector. Requires both ligand binding (glutamate) AND postsynaptic depolarization to remove Mg2+ block. Critical for LTP, synaptic plasticity, and learning. Blocked by Mg2+ at rest.
concepts.keywords: coincidence detector, Mg2+ block, glutamate, depolarization, LTP, synaptic plasticity, AMPA, calcium influx

concepts.name: Narcissism
concepts.keywords: narcissism, self-psychology, Kohut, narcissistic personality, grandiosity

concepts.name: Narcissistic Personality Disorder
concepts.def: Grandiosity, need for admiration, lack of empathy; fragile self-esteem; social cognitive model (Morf & Rhodewalt); difficult to treat; schema-focused therapy.
concepts.keywords: narcissistic, NPD, grandiosity, empathy, Kohut, Morf, Rhodewalt, social cognitive model, DSM-5

concepts.name: Nature-Nurture Interactions
concepts.keywords: nature, nurture, gene-environment interaction, shared environment, nonshared environment

concepts.name: Neglect Syndrome & Balint Syndrome
concepts.def: Neglect: failure to attend to one side of space (usually left) after right parietal/temporal damage. Patient draws only right half of objects, ignores left-side food, etc. Balint Syndrome: bilateral parietal damage causing simultanagnosia (see only one object), optic ataxia (misreaching), and ocular apraxia (cannot voluntarily direct gaze).
concepts.keywords: right parietal cortex, hemispatial neglect, simultanagnosia, optic ataxia, ocular apraxia, ventral attention network, line cancellation test, representational neglect
concepts.mnemonic: Neglect: right parietal damage NEGLECTS the left world. Balint = BALance is gone: can only see ONE object, misreach, lose eye control.

concepts.name: Neglect Syndrome (Hemispatial Neglect)
concepts.def: Failure to attend to contralateral (usually left) space, typically after right parietal damage. Patient doesn't copy left side of drawing, eats only right side of plate, denies left arm exists. Distinct from blindness — is a failure of attention, not vision.
concepts.keywords: right parietal, contralateral, spatial attention, line bisection, drawing task, Bálint's syndrome, anosognosia, extinction
concepts.mnemonic: Neglect = the brain saying Not Existing Left. Right parietal damage makes left space invisible to attention.

concepts.name: Neurocognitive Disorders (Major and Mild NCD, Delirium)
concepts.def: Acquired decline from a previous level of cognitive functioning. Major NCD (dementia) = significant decline + impaired independence. Mild NCD = modest decline + preserved independence.
concepts.keywords: NCD, dementia, Alzheimer's, Lewy body, frontotemporal, delirium, major vs mild, IADLs, acquired cognitive decline
concepts.mnemonic: Major NCD = Significant decline + Can't do IADLs. Mild NCD = Modest decline + Still independent. Delirium = ACUTE fluctuating reversible

concepts.name: Neuropsychology Key Case Studies
concepts.def: Classic cases: H.M. (bilateral hippocampectomy, anterograde amnesia); Phineas Gage (vmPFC, personality change); P.T. / M.P. (MT damage, akinetopsia); D.F. (ventral stream, visual agnosia); G.S. (visual agnosia, can copy but not name); S.M. (bilateral amygdala, no fear); E.V.R. (OFC, poor decisions).
concepts.keywords: H.M., Phineas Gage, D.F., G.S., S.M., E.V.R., case study, neuropsychology, lesion, brain-behavior
concepts.mnemonic: HPGSE: H.M.(memory), Phineas(personality), G.S.(object agnosia), S.M.(no fear), E.V.R.(bad decisions). Each = one specific deficit from one specific region.

concepts.name: Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder (OCD)
concepts.def: Recurrent obsessions and/or compulsions causing distress; classified under OCD-related disorders in DSM-5; neurobiological etiology (orbitofrontal-striatal circuit); treated with ERP and SSRIs.
concepts.keywords: OCD, obsessions, compulsions, ERP, exposure and response prevention, SSRIs, orbitofrontal cortex, caudate nucleus, DSM-5

concepts.name: Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder (OCD)
concepts.def: A disorder characterized by time-consuming obsessions (recurrent intrusive thoughts) and/or compulsions (repetitive behaviors/mental acts), causing distress or functional impairment.
concepts.keywords: OCD, obsessions, compulsions, ego-dystonic, ERP, insight specifier, checking, contamination, OCPD differential

concepts.keywords: opiates, opioids, heroin, methadone, buprenorphine, Suboxone, naltrexone, opioid use disorder, withdrawal, mu receptor

concepts.name: Optogenetics
concepts.def: Genetic technique introducing light-sensitive proteins (channelrhodopsins) into specific neurons, allowing their activity to be controlled with light pulses with millisecond precision. Can activate (ChR2) or inhibit (halorhodopsin) specific cell types in awake behaving animals.
concepts.keywords: channelrhodopsin, ChR2, halorhodopsin, light-gated ion channel, cell-type specific, millisecond precision, causal, Boyden, Deisseroth
concepts.mnemonic: Optogenetics = Light switch for neurons. Blue light ON = channelrhodopsin opens = neuron fires.

concepts.name: Oral Stage of Development
concepts.keywords: Freud, oral stage, psychosexual stages, oral personality, dependency, fixation

concepts.name: PET (Positron Emission Tomography)
concepts.def: Neuroimaging method using radioactive tracers (e.g., 15O-water for blood flow, 18F-FDG for glucose). Lower spatial resolution than fMRI, true metabolic measurement. Used for neurotransmitter receptor mapping, Alzheimer beta-amyloid imaging (PiB).
concepts.keywords: radioactive tracer, 15O-water, FDG, blood flow, glucose metabolism, amyloid imaging, PiB, Alzheimer

concepts.name: PTSD (Posttraumatic Stress Disorder)
concepts.def: DSM-5: Exposure to traumatic event + Cluster B (intrusion), C (avoidance), D (negative cognitions/mood), E (arousal/reactivity) lasting >1 month. Moved from anxiety to Trauma-Related Disorders in DSM-5.
concepts.keywords: trauma, intrusion, avoidance, hyperarousal, flashbacks, HPA axis, EMDR, prolonged exposure, cortisol, amygdala, hippocampus
concepts.mnemonic: PTSD clusters B-C-D-E: Be Careful Dodging Exposure. DSM-5 moved PTSD OUT of anxiety disorders chapter.

concepts.name: Panic Disorder
concepts.def: Recurrent unexpected panic attacks plus persistent concern or behavioral change; fear-of-fear cycle; neurobiological and cognitive models; treated with CBT (PCT) and SSRIs.
concepts.keywords: panic disorder, panic attack, unexpected, fear-of-fear, agoraphobia, PCT, CBT, locus ceruleus, interoceptive conditioning

concepts.name: Panic Disorder
concepts.def: Recurrent unexpected panic attacks followed by >=1 month of persistent concern about attacks or maladaptive behavioral changes.
concepts.keywords: panic disorder, unexpected panic attack, 4 symptoms, minutes, anticipatory anxiety, agoraphobia, palpitations, derealization
concepts.mnemonic: 4 in minutes - 4 symptoms peak in minutes. UNEXPECTED attacks = key feature of Panic Disorder

concepts.name: Parietal Lobe Functions
concepts.def: Anterior: primary somatosensory cortex (BA 1, 2, 3). Posterior parietal: visuospatial processing, spatial attention, sensorimotor integration. IPS: object manipulation, spatial location. Damage: neglect (right), apraxia, optic ataxia, Balint's syndrome. Junction point between sensory and motor systems.
concepts.keywords: somatosensory, posterior parietal, IPS, spatial attention, apraxia, optic ataxia, Balint's syndrome, neglect, sensorimotor integration

concepts.name: Parkinson's Disease
concepts.def: Progressive neurodegenerative disorder caused by loss of dopaminergic neurons in substantia nigra pars compacta, reducing striatal dopamine. Motor symptoms: tremor at rest, rigidity, bradykinesia, postural instability. MPTP model in monkeys. Treatment: L-dopa, deep brain stimulation (STN/GPi).
concepts.keywords: substantia nigra, dopamine, bradykinesia, rigidity, tremor, MPTP, L-dopa, deep brain stimulation, basal ganglia, nigrostriatal pathway
concepts.mnemonic: Parkinson's = Paralyzed by dopamine loss. TRAP: Tremor at rest, Rigidity, Akinesia/bradykinesia, Postural instability. Treat with L-dopa (dopamine precursor).

concepts.mnemonic: A=MAD (odd), B=BAD (dramatic), C=SAD (anxious). BPD 5/9 criteria: splitting, abandonment, self-harm, identity disturbance

concepts.name: Personality Disorders Overview (DSM-5)
concepts.def: Enduring maladaptive patterns of inner experience and behavior; DSM-5 retains 10 types in 3 clusters (A: odd, B: dramatic, C: anxious); also proposes alternative dimensional model.

concepts.name: Phineas Gage Case
concepts.def: Classic 1848 case: railroad worker survived iron rod through left frontal lobe (specifically vmPFC/OFC). Before: responsible and well-liked. After: profane, socially inappropriate, unable to plan. Damasio: vmPFC damage disrupts somatic markers needed for social decision-making. Preserved language and IQ.
concepts.keywords: vmPFC, OFC, personality change, social decision-making, tamping rod, frontal lobe, Damasio, somatic marker, social behavior
concepts.mnemonic: Phineas Gage = Personality Gone after frontal lobe damage. Smart but socially shattered. The vmPFC connects emotion to decision-making.

concepts.name: Planum Temporale Asymmetry
concepts.def: Triangular region on upper surface of superior temporal gyrus (posterior Heschl's). Larger in left hemisphere (67% of people, including most right-handers). Corresponds anatomically to Wernicke's area. Proposed as anatomical basis for left hemisphere language dominance. Found in some non-human primates.
concepts.keywords: planum temporale, temporal lobe asymmetry, Wernicke's area, left hemisphere, language dominance, Geschwind, Levitsky, auditory cortex

concepts.name: Positive Psychology Movement
concepts.keywords: Seligman, positive psychology, flow, human strengths, virtues, well-being

concepts.name: Posttraumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD)
concepts.def: Trauma-related disorder: intrusion, avoidance, negative cognition/mood, hyperarousal lasting >1 month; treated with Prolonged Exposure, CPT, EMDR, SSRIs.
concepts.keywords: PTSD, trauma, intrusion, avoidance, hyperarousal, prolonged exposure, CPT, EMDR, Foa, Resick, DSM-5 clusters

concepts.name: Posttraumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD)
concepts.def: A disorder following exposure to a traumatic event, characterized by intrusion symptoms, avoidance, negative alterations in cognitions/mood, and alterations in arousal/reactivity, lasting >1 month.
concepts.keywords: PTSD, intrusion, avoidance, hyperarousal, negative cognitions, flashback, 1 month, dissociative specifier, acute stress disorder
concepts.mnemonic: I-ACE: Intrusion, Avoidance, Cognition/mood (negative), Excitation/arousal. DSM-5 has 4 clusters (was 3 in DSM-IV)
concepts.page: Section II - Trauma- and Stressor-Related Disorders

concepts.name: Prefrontal Cortex (PFC)
concepts.def: Anterior frontal lobe. Roles: working memory (dlPFC), decision-making (vmPFC/OFC), cognitive control, goal-directed behavior, inhibition of action. Phineas Gage: vmPFC damage -> personality change. Matures last (~25 years). Goldman-Rakic: PFC and working memory.
concepts.keywords: working memory, cognitive control, dlPFC, vmPFC, OFC, Phineas Gage, decision making, goal-oriented behavior, maturation
concepts.mnemonic: PFC = Planning, Flexibility, Control. The brain's CEO — last to mature, first to show personality when damaged.

concepts.name: Prefrontal Cortex (PFC)
concepts.def: Frontal lobe region mediating executive functions, emotion regulation, and decision-making; hypoactive in schizophrenia (dorsolateral PFC) and depression; impaired in substance use disorders and BPD.
concepts.keywords: prefrontal cortex, PFC, executive function, emotion regulation, schizophrenia, dorsolateral, orbitofrontal, depression, BPD

concepts.name: Primary Motor Cortex (M1) and Motor Homunculus
concepts.def: Precentral gyrus (BA4). Somatotopic organization (motor homunculus: Penfield). Corticospinal tract originates here. Georgopoulos: neurons show directional tuning, population vectors code movement direction. Damage causes contralateral spastic paralysis.
concepts.keywords: motor homunculus, Penfield, BA4, precentral gyrus, corticospinal tract, directional tuning, population vector, Georgopoulos, somatotopy
concepts.mnemonic: M1 = Movement maker. Homunculus: face at bottom, feet at top of precentral gyrus. Hands get huge representation.

concepts.name: Priming (Perceptual and Conceptual)
concepts.def: Prior exposure to a stimulus facilitates subsequent processing of that or related stimuli. Perceptual priming: faster identification of physically similar stimuli (visual cortex). Conceptual/semantic priming: faster processing of semantically related concepts (bilateral). Spared in amnesia: does NOT require hippocampus.
concepts.keywords: semantic priming, perceptual priming, implicit memory, spared amnesia, repetition priming, facilitation, lexical decision, word fragment completion

concepts.name: Procedural Memory (Nondeclarative Memory)
concepts.def: Implicit, non-conscious memory for skills and habits (riding a bike, mirror tracing), classical conditioning, and priming. Does NOT depend on hippocampus. Spared in H.M. Depends on basal ganglia (habits), cerebellum (motor skills), and amygdala (fear conditioning).
concepts.keywords: implicit memory, skills, habits, classical conditioning, priming, basal ganglia, cerebellum, H.M. spared, mirror tracing
concepts.mnemonic: Procedural = Proceed without thinking. Your hands know how to type even when you can't explain it. H.M. could learn mirror tracing!

concepts.name: Projection (Defense Mechanism)
concepts.keywords: defense mechanism, projection, Freud, attributing feelings to others

concepts.name: Projective Tests

concepts.name: Prosopagnosia
concepts.def: Inability to recognize faces despite intact visual acuity. Associated with damage to right fusiform face area (fusiform gyrus). Can be acquired (stroke) or developmental (congenital). Patients recognize by voice, gait, clothing. Cannot recognize famous faces or own face in mirror.
concepts.keywords: fusiform face area, right hemisphere, face recognition, FFA, developmental prosopagnosia, acquired prosopagnosia, holistic processing
concepts.mnemonic: Prosopagnosia = Prosopon (face) + agnosia (not knowing). Can't recognize FACES. Think Brad Pitt couldn't recognize Brad Pitt.

concepts.name: Psychoanalysis / Psychodynamic Theory
concepts.def: Freud's theory of unconscious conflicts, psychosexual development, and defense mechanisms as determinants of behavior; Neo-Freudians (Adler, Jung, Horney) extended this; psychodynamic therapy derived from it.
concepts.keywords: Freud, psychoanalysis, unconscious, id ego superego, defense mechanisms, psychosexual stages, transference, free association, Adler, Jung

concepts.name: Retinotopic Organization
concepts.def: Topographic mapping where neighboring points in the visual field are represented by neighboring neurons in visual cortex. Present from retina through LGN to V1 and beyond. Foveal magnification: disproportionately large representation of central visual field in V1.
concepts.keywords: topographic map, foveal magnification, V1, retinotopy, LGN, visual field, spatial representation

concepts.name: Reward Prediction Error
concepts.def: Dopamine neurons fire for unexpected rewards (positive prediction error), are suppressed by expected rewards that don't occur (negative prediction error), and don't respond to fully expected rewards. Schultz's temporal difference learning model. Key mechanism of reinforcement learning in basal ganglia.
concepts.keywords: Schultz, dopamine, temporal difference learning, reward, punishment, VTA, striatum, reinforcement learning, wanting vs liking, Berridge
concepts.mnemonic: Prediction Error: DA fires for SURPRISE reward. Expected reward = no DA response. Worse than expected = DA DROPS below baseline.

concepts.name: Schizoaffective Disorder
concepts.def: A disorder with concurrent features of schizophrenia and a major mood episode, where psychotic symptoms also occur without mood symptoms for >=2 weeks, and mood symptoms dominate the total illness duration.
concepts.keywords: schizoaffective, bipolar type, depressive type, 2 weeks psychosis without mood, majority mood, differential from schizophrenia
concepts.mnemonic: SAD = Schizo + Affect + Duration. Psychosis without mood >=2 weeks + Affect dominates total duration
concepts.page: Section II - Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders

concepts.name: Schizophrenia
concepts.def: Psychotic disorder characterized by positive symptoms (hallucinations, delusions), negative symptoms (flat affect, avolition), and disorganized symptoms; high heritability; dopamine/glutamate etiology.
concepts.keywords: schizophrenia, positive symptoms, negative symptoms, hallucinations, delusions, flat affect, avolition, dopamine, glutamate, antipsychotics, DSM-5

concepts.name: Schizophrenia
concepts.def: A psychotic disorder characterized by delusions, hallucinations, disorganized speech, disorganized behavior, and negative symptoms, lasting >= 6 months with significant functional decline.
concepts.keywords: schizophrenia, positive symptoms, negative symptoms, delusions, hallucinations, 6 months, dopamine, alogia, avolition, anhedonia
concepts.mnemonic: 1-6-A: 1 month active symptoms, 6 months total duration, At least 1 of delusions/hallucinations/disorganized speech
concepts.page: Section II - Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders

concepts.name: Schizotypal Personality Disorder
concepts.def: Schizophrenia spectrum disorder; odd beliefs, magical thinking, ideas of reference, unusual perceptual experiences; poor social functioning; genetic overlap with schizophrenia.
concepts.keywords: schizotypal, schizophrenia spectrum, odd beliefs, magical thinking, ideas of reference, genetic, DSM-5, Cluster A

concepts.name: Selective Attention
concepts.def: The ability to selectively process one stimulus or location while ignoring others. Two varieties: voluntary (endogenous, top-down, goal-driven) and reflexive (exogenous, bottom-up, stimulus-driven). Dorsal attention network (voluntary) vs. ventral attention network (reflexive reorienting).
concepts.keywords: endogenous, exogenous, top-down, bottom-up, dorsal attention network, ventral attention network, cocktail party effect, spatial attention
concepts.mnemonic: Selective Attention = spotlight on ONE thing. Voluntary (TOP-down you choose) vs Reflexive (BOTTOM-up something grabs you).

concepts.name: Selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs)
concepts.zh: 選擇性血清素回收抑制劑
concepts.def: 阻止神經細胞回收已釋放的血清素，讓血清素在細胞間隙中停留更久、作用更強，進而幫助大腦恢復正常運作。

concepts.name: Self-Referential Processing
concepts.def: Processing information in reference to oneself. Activates medial PFC, posterior cingulate, and other default network regions. Self-reference effect: information encoded with reference to self is better remembered. Includes self-perception, body ownership (rubber hand illusion), out-of-body experiences, and theory of mind.
concepts.keywords: medial PFC, default network, self-reference effect, posterior cingulate, body ownership, rubber hand illusion, out-of-body, memory encoding

concepts.keywords: serotonin, 5-HT, SSRI, monoamine, depression, OCD, anxiety, eating disorders, 5-HTT, gene-environment interaction, suicide

concepts.keywords: serotonin, neurotransmitter, mood, temperament, socioeconomic status, brain plasticity

concepts.name: Single and Double Dissociation
concepts.def: Single dissociation: damage to area A impairs task X but not Y (suggests A contributes to X). Double dissociation: damage to A impairs X not Y; damage to B impairs Y not X. Double dissociation is stronger evidence for functional independence of two processes.
concepts.keywords: lesion method, functional independence, neuropsychology, patient studies, causal inference, brain-behavior relationship
concepts.mnemonic: Double dissociation = Double proof. A breaks X not Y; B breaks Y not X. Now we're SURE they're separate.

concepts.keywords: six-factor model, HEXACO, Ashton, Lee, honesty-humility, personality traits

concepts.name: Sleep Stages, Disorders & Neural Mechanisms
concepts.def: 4 sleep stages: NREM 1-3 (delta waves in SWS) and REM. REM = pons. SCN controls circadian rhythms. Narcolepsy = hypocretin deficiency.
concepts.keywords: slow-wave sleep, REM, NREM, delta waves, narcolepsy, hypocretin, sleep paralysis, sleep apnea, SCN, suprachiasmatic nucleus, pons, activation-synthesis
concepts.mnemonic: NREM gets DEEPER: Stage 1 (light) → Stage 2 (spindles) → Stage 3 (Delta/deep). REM = Random Excitement of Motor neurons (suppressed)

concepts.name: Social (Pragmatic) Communication Disorder
concepts.def: Persistent difficulties in the social use of verbal and nonverbal communication, not better explained by IDD, language disorder, or ASD.
concepts.keywords: social pragmatic communication disorder, SPCD, pragmatics, social use, ASD differential, no RRBs, DSM-5 new
concepts.mnemonic: SPCD = Social use impaired + No RRBs. ASD = Social use impaired + RRBs present
concepts.page: Section II - Neurodevelopmental Disorders - Communication Disorders

concepts.name: Social Anxiety Disorder (Social Phobia)
concepts.def: Marked fear of social situations with negative evaluation; avoidance; treated with CBT (exposure, cognitive restructuring, social skills training) and SSRIs; often comorbid with APD.
concepts.keywords: social anxiety disorder, social phobia, fear negative evaluation, CBT, exposure, SSRIs, taijin kyofusho, DSM-5, avoidant PD

concepts.name: Social Domain Theory (Moral Development)
concepts.def: Three moral domains: Moral imperatives (universal, harm-based), Social conventions (context-specific norms), Personal choice (discretionary). Turiel & Smetana.
concepts.keywords: social domain theory, moral imperatives, social conventions, personal choice, moral development, Turiel, Smetana, inductive discipline
concepts.mnemonic: MSP = Moral (universal), Social (group), Personal (mine) — from widest to narrowest obligation

concepts.keywords: Carstensen, socioemotional selectivity, older adults, emotional goals, time horizon

concepts.name: Somatic Marker Hypothesis
concepts.def: Damasio's theory: emotions guide decision-making via bodily states. vmPFC integrates bodily states with decision context. Evidence: E.V.R. (vmPFC damage) had normal IQ but catastrophic life decisions. Iowa Gambling Task: vmPFC patients fail to learn advantageous choices.
concepts.keywords: Damasio, vmPFC, Iowa Gambling Task, E.V.R., Phineas Gage, emotion and decision-making, insula, bodily state, skin conductance
concepts.mnemonic: Somatic Markers = body feelings guiding choices. Gut feeling = vmPFC integrating past emotional outcomes. No vmPFC = gambling without learning.

concepts.name: Somatic Symptom Disorder (Complex)
concepts.def: Distressing or disruptive somatic symptoms accompanied by excessive thoughts, feelings, or behaviors related to health; DSM-5 replacement for somatization disorder; treated with CBT.
concepts.keywords: somatic symptom disorder, somatization, CBT, health anxiety, DSM-5, somatic, bodily distress, hypochondriasis

concepts.name: Spatial Attention Networks (Dorsal / Ventral)
concepts.def: Dorsal Attention Network (DAN): IPS + FEF, voluntary/endogenous top-down control of spatial attention. Ventral Attention Network (VAN): right TPJ + right VFC, reflexive/exogenous reorienting to unexpected stimuli. Corbetta and Shulman model. Damage to right VAN causes neglect. Two networks interact.
concepts.keywords: IPS, FEF, TPJ, right hemisphere, dorsal attention network, ventral attention network, voluntary, reflexive, neglect, Corbetta, Shulman
concepts.mnemonic: DAN = Deliberate Attention Network (you choose where to look). VAN = Vigilance Alert Network (something grabs you). Right VAN damage = left neglect.

concepts.name: Spatial Memory and Place Cells
concepts.def: Place cells (O'Keefe): hippocampal neurons firing selectively when animal occupies specific location (place field). Grid cells (Moser): entorhinal cortex, fire in hexagonal grid pattern providing metric for space. Together create cognitive map of environment. Disrupted by hippocampal lesions. Basis of Nobel Prize 2014.
concepts.keywords: place cells, grid cells, O'Keefe, Moser, cognitive map, hippocampus, entorhinal cortex, spatial navigation, Morris water maze

concepts.name: Specific Phobia
concepts.def: Marked fear/anxiety about specific object or situation; immediate fear response; avoidance; five subtypes (animal, natural environment, blood-injection-injury, situational, other).
concepts.keywords: specific phobia, DSM-5, five subtypes, exposure therapy, systematic desensitization, BII subtype, vasovagal response

concepts.name: Split-Brain Research
concepts.def: Studies of patients whose corpus callosum was surgically severed for epilepsy treatment (callosotomy). Revealed functional lateralization: left hemisphere = language, right = visuospatial. Gazzaniga's interpreter theory: left hemisphere generates narrative explanations. Key patients: W.J., J.W., P.S.
concepts.keywords: callosotomy, hemispheric specialization, left hemisphere language, right visuospatial, interpreter, W.J., J.W., lateralization
concepts.mnemonic: Split-brain shows the split roles: LEFT=Language/Logic; RIGHT=Recognition/visuoSpatial. The left interpreter makes up stories for what the right brain did.

concepts.name: Stereotype Threat
concepts.keywords: Steele, stereotype threat, performance, stigma, identity, social identity

concepts.name: Strange Situation Procedure
concepts.keywords: Ainsworth, strange situation, attachment, secure, anxious-ambivalent, anxious-avoidant

concepts.name: Substance Use Disorders (General Overview)
concepts.def: DSM-5 combines abuse and dependence into single SUD; criteria include tolerance, withdrawal, loss of control; etiology: genetic, neurobiological (reward pathway), psychological, sociocultural.
concepts.keywords: substance use disorder, DSM-5, tolerance, withdrawal, reward pathway, dopamine, genetic, sociocultural, prevention

concepts.name: Substance Use Disorders (Overview)
concepts.def: A problematic pattern of substance use causing clinically significant impairment or distress, diagnosed by >=2 criteria in 12 months across 4 domains: impaired control, social impairment, risky use, pharmacological criteria.
concepts.keywords: SUD, substance use disorder, craving, tolerance, withdrawal, alcohol withdrawal, DTs, opioid, CAGE, DSM-5 change abuse dependence
concepts.mnemonic: CAGE screening: Cut down, Annoyed, Guilty, Eye-opener. DTs = most dangerous withdrawal (benzodiazepines). Opioid withdrawal = miserable not lethal
concepts.page: Section II - Substance-Related and Addictive Disorders

concepts.name: Suicide: Epidemiology, Models & Prevention
concepts.def: Leading cause of death; models: neurobiological (serotonin), psychological (hopelessness, Joiner's interpersonal theory), sociological (Durkheim); prevention via means restriction, crisis lines, CBT.
concepts.keywords: suicide, suicidal ideation, hopelessness, Joiner, interpersonal theory, thwarted belonging, perceived burdensomeness, Durkheim, prevention, means restriction

concepts.keywords: interpersonal psychiatry, self-system, anxiety, parataxic distortion

concepts.name: Superego
concepts.keywords: Freud, superego, conscience, ego ideal, moral standards, guilt

concepts.name: Synaptic Transmission
concepts.def: Communication across the synapse. Action potential arrives at axon terminal -> Ca2+ influx -> vesicle fusion -> neurotransmitter release into cleft -> binds postsynaptic receptors -> EPSP or IPSP -> reuptake or enzymatic degradation.
concepts.keywords: neurotransmitter, vesicle, Ca2+, EPSP, IPSP, reuptake, receptor, agonist, antagonist, chemical synapse

concepts.name: Systematic Desensitization
concepts.def: Wolpe's behavioral technique: gradual exposure to feared stimuli paired with relaxation; based on counter-conditioning/reciprocal inhibition; effective for phobias.
concepts.keywords: systematic desensitization, Wolpe, reciprocal inhibition, relaxation, anxiety hierarchy, counter-conditioning, phobia, in vivo, imaginal

concepts.name: Systematic Desensitization
concepts.keywords: Wolpe, systematic desensitization, phobia, classical conditioning, hierarchy of fears

concepts.name: Systematic Desensitization (Wolpe)
concepts.def: Wolpe's behavioral treatment for phobias. Three steps: (1) relaxation training, (2) anxiety hierarchy construction, (3) imaginal pairing of relaxation with graded anxiety stimuli. Based on reciprocal inhibition.
concepts.keywords: Wolpe, reciprocal inhibition, anxiety hierarchy, in vivo vs imaginal, counterconditioning, phobias, SUDS
concepts.mnemonic: DESENSITIZE in 3 steps: Relax + Rank fears + Rehearse pairing. Wolpe's 3R recipe.

concepts.name: TMS (Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation)
concepts.def: Noninvasive brain stimulation using magnetic pulses to induce electrical currents in cortex. Creates temporary virtual lesions (single-pulse) or modulates excitability (rTMS). Used to test causal role of brain regions. Gold standard for causality in cognitive neuroscience.
concepts.keywords: virtual lesion, causal inference, rTMS, single-pulse TMS, motor evoked potential, stimulation mapping, causality
concepts.mnemonic: TMS = Temporarily Mutes Spots. A virtual lesion to test if an area is NECESSARY.

concepts.name: Tarasoff Duty to Warn
concepts.def: Tarasoff v. Regents of UC (1976): Therapists have a legal duty to warn identifiable potential victims when a patient makes a credible threat of violence. Confidentiality yields to public safety.
concepts.keywords: confidentiality, duty to protect, identifiable victim, civil commitment, privileged communication, Jablonski v. US
concepts.mnemonic: TARASOFF = Tell About Risk And Save Others From harm. Safety overrides secrets.

concepts.name: Tarasoff Duty to Warn
concepts.def: Therapists have a duty to warn identifiable third parties of serious threats made by clients; established in Tarasoff v. Regents of UC; extends confidentiality limits.
concepts.keywords: Tarasoff, duty to warn, duty to protect, confidentiality, Poddar, third party, identifiable victim, California

concepts.name: Temperament
concepts.keywords: temperament, biological foundations, inhibition, effortful control, Kagan, New York Longitudinal Study

concepts.name: Terror Management Theory
concepts.keywords: Greenberg, Solomon, Pyszczynski, terror management, mortality salience, death anxiety, worldview

concepts.name: Thalamus
concepts.def: Diencephalic relay center. Almost all sensory information (except olfaction) passes through thalamus to cortex. Key nuclei: LGN (vision), MGN (audition), VPL/VPM (somatosensation), pulvinar (attention). Thalamic reticular nucleus modulates attention. Also involved in consciousness regulation.
concepts.keywords: LGN, MGN, VPL, pulvinar, thalamic reticular nucleus, relay center, diencephalon, sensory gating, attention, consciousness
concepts.mnemonic: Thalamus = The Toll booth. All sensory info (except smell) must pass through to reach cortex.

concepts.name: The Interpreter (Gazzaniga)
concepts.def: Left hemisphere mechanism that continuously generates causal explanations for behavior, even when the real cause is unknown. Demonstrated in split-brain patients: left hemisphere confabulates explanations for actions controlled by the right hemisphere. The interpreter creates the unified sense of self and narrative identity.
concepts.keywords: Gazzaniga, split-brain, confabulation, left hemisphere, narrative self, explanatory mechanism, callosotomy, J.W., causation, motivated reasoning
concepts.mnemonic: The INTERPRETER is always talking, even when it doesn't know the truth. Left hemisphere making up stories = why we feel our life has narrative coherence.

concepts.name: Thematic Apperception Test (TAT)
concepts.keywords: TAT, projective test, Murray, achievement motivation, unconscious needs

concepts.name: Theory of Mind (ToM)
concepts.def: The ability to attribute mental states (beliefs, desires, intentions) to oneself and others and to understand that others can have different mental states than you. Tested with false-belief tasks (Sally-Anne). Deficit in ASD. Neural substrates: TPJ, MPFC, STS, temporal pole.
concepts.keywords: Premack, Woodruff, Baron-Cohen, Sally-Anne task, false belief, ASD mindblindness, TPJ, MPFC, mentalizing, simulation theory vs. theory theory
concepts.mnemonic: ToM = reading minds by modeling their beliefs. Sally-Anne test: does the child know Sally has a FALSE belief about where the marble is? ASD children fail at age 4 while typical children pass.
concepts.theorists: Premack, Woodruff, Baron-Cohen, Wimmer, Perner, Saxe

concepts.name: Theory of Mind (ToM)
concepts.def: Ability to attribute mental states (beliefs, desires, intentions) to others and understand they may differ from one's own. Assessed with false-belief tasks (Sally-Anne). Neural basis: medial PFC, TPJ, STS. Impaired in autism spectrum disorder. Develops around age 4 in typically developing children.
concepts.keywords: mentalizing, false-belief task, Sally-Anne, MPFC, TPJ, STS, autism, mirror neurons, mentalization, age 4
concepts.mnemonic: ToM = 'Theory of Others' Minds. Sally-Anne test: does the child know Sally will look where SHE thinks the ball is (not where it actually is)?

concepts.name: Theory of Mind (ToM) Development
concepts.def: Ability to attribute mental states to self and others; understanding others can hold false beliefs. Passes false-belief task ~age 4.
concepts.keywords: theory of mind, false-belief task, Sally-Anne, perspective-taking, egocentric thinking, inhibitory control, mental states, deception
concepts.mnemonic: ToM at Age 4: I know OTHERS have DIFFERENT minds from mine
concepts.theorists: Premack & Woodruff, Wimmer & Perner, Baron-Cohen, Wellman

concepts.name: Tobacco Use Disorder
concepts.def: Nicotine dependence from cigarette/tobacco use; highly addictive; major health consequences; treated with nicotine replacement, varenicline, bupropion, behavioral counseling.
concepts.keywords: tobacco, nicotine, smoking, varenicline, Chantix, bupropion, Wellbutrin, NRT, health consequences, cessation

concepts.name: Twin Studies
concepts.keywords: twin studies, behavioral genetics, MZ vs DZ twins, heritability, identical twins

concepts.name: Twin Studies (Behavior Genetics)
concepts.def: Compare concordance rates in MZ (identical) vs. DZ (fraternal) twins to estimate heritability of disorders; MZ > DZ concordance suggests genetic influence; MZ < 100% confirms environmental contribution.
concepts.keywords: twin study, MZ, DZ, concordance, heritability, behavior genetics, monozygotic, dizygotic, adoption study

concepts.name: Unconditional Positive Regard
concepts.keywords: Rogers, unconditional positive regard, therapeutic conditions, acceptance, client-centered

concepts.name: Visual Agnosia
concepts.def: Inability to recognize objects visually despite adequate visual acuity. Two types: apperceptive (can't form normal percept) vs. associative (percept intact but can't match to stored knowledge). Category-specific: living things vs. artifacts. Associated with ventral stream damage. Cases: G.S., C.K., J.B.R.
concepts.keywords: apperceptive agnosia, associative agnosia, category-specific, ventral stream, IT cortex, living vs nonliving, G.S., D.F., object recognition
concepts.mnemonic: Agnosia = A + gnosis (without knowledge). Can't recognize despite seeing. Apperceptive = can't COPY. Associative = can COPY but can't NAME.

concepts.name: Visual Cortex Organization (V1-V5/MT)
concepts.def: V1 (primary visual cortex, BA17): retinotopic map, orientation columns, simple and complex cells (Hubel & Wiesel). V2/V3: spatial frequency, depth. V4: color processing; damage = achromatopsia. MT/V5: motion perception; damage = akinetopsia (can't see movement).
concepts.keywords: V1, V4, MT/V5, retinotopic map, orientation tuning, ocular dominance columns, achromatopsia, akinetopsia, Hubel, Wiesel
concepts.mnemonic: V4 = Color (4 letters = hue). MT/V5 = Motion (M for Movement). No MT = can't see Motion (akinetopsia).

concepts.name: Watson, John B.
concepts.keywords: behaviorism, Little Albert, conditioning, observable behavior, stimulus-response

concepts.name: Wernicke's Area
concepts.def: Left posterior superior temporal gyrus (BA 22). Critical for language comprehension and word recognition. Damage causes Wernicke's aphasia: fluent but meaningless speech (word salad) with poor comprehension. Part of left perisylvian language network.
concepts.keywords: BA 22, language comprehension, word recognition, Wernicke's aphasia, fluent aphasia, word salad, neologisms, paraphasias
concepts.mnemonic: Wernicke's = WORDS without MEANING. Fluent but nonsensical. Can produce speech but can't understand it.

concepts.name: Wernicke-Lichtheim Model of Language
concepts.def: Classic connectionist model of language. Wernicke's area = auditory word images; Broca's area = motor speech patterns. Connection between them (arcuate fasciculus) = repetition. Lesion at each node or connection predicts different aphasia type. Geschwind updated with disconnection syndromes.
concepts.keywords: Wernicke, Broca, arcuate fasciculus, disconnection, aphasia classification, Lichtheim, Geschwind, conduction aphasia, language model

concepts.name: Working Memory
concepts.def: Active short-term maintenance and manipulation of information. Baddeley's model: phonological loop (verbal), visuospatial sketchpad (visual), episodic buffer (integrates), central executive (controls). Depends on dlPFC. Capacity ~4 items (not Miller's magical 7 plus/minus 2).
concepts.keywords: Baddeley, phonological loop, visuospatial sketchpad, central executive, episodic buffer, PFC, dlPFC, capacity, Miller's 7
concepts.mnemonic: Working Memory = RAM in the brain. Baddeley's 4 components: Phonological Loop + Visuospatial Sketchpad + Episodic Buffer + Central Executive.

concepts.name: Working Memory (Baddeley Model)
concepts.def: Baddeley and Hitch (1974): Working memory has three components: (1) Central Executive (attentional controller), (2) Phonological Loop (verbal/acoustic storage and rehearsal), (3) Visuospatial Sketchpad (visual and spatial information). Later added episodic buffer. Prefrontal cortex critical for working memory maintenance.
concepts.keywords: Baddeley, central executive, phonological loop, visuospatial sketchpad, episodic buffer, Miller magic number 7, prefrontal cortex, short-term memory
concepts.mnemonic: Working memory = CEO (Central Executive) + phone (Phonological Loop) + sketch (Visuospatial Sketchpad). Think: the CEO uses a phone and a sketchpad.

concepts.name: Wyatt v. Stickney (Right to Treatment)
concepts.def: Landmark 1971 Alabama case establishing that civilly committed patients have a constitutional right to adequate treatment; set standards for psychiatric hospital conditions.
concepts.keywords: Wyatt v. Stickney, right to treatment, civil commitment, constitutional, Alabama, psychiatric hospital standards

concepts.name: fMRI (Functional MRI) Principles
concepts.def: Measures BOLD (blood oxygen level-dependent) signal as proxy for neural activity. When neurons fire, local blood flow increases (hemodynamic response). Superior spatial resolution vs. EEG/MEG. Poor temporal resolution (seconds). Indirect measure. Cannot establish causality alone. Developed by Ogawa (1990), Kwong and Raichle.
concepts.keywords: BOLD signal, hemodynamic response, spatial resolution, temporal resolution, indirect measure, deoxyhemoglobin, Ogawa, fMRI limitations, neuroimaging
concepts.mnemonic: fMRI = Fired neurons Need More blood. BOLD = Blood Oxygen Level Dependent. Good SPACE, bad TIME. Cannot prove causation (use TMS for that).

concepts.name: fMRI (Functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging)
concepts.def: Neuroimaging method measuring BOLD (blood oxygen level-dependent) signal. Detects changes in oxygenated vs. deoxygenated hemoglobin as a proxy for neural activity. High spatial resolution (~mm), poor temporal resolution (~seconds). Most widely used human neuroimaging.
concepts.keywords: BOLD, hemoglobin, spatial resolution, temporal resolution, hemodynamic response, block design, event-related design, limitations
concepts.mnemonic: fMRI = Finding Metabolic Responses Indirectly. Measures blood O2 changes, not neurons directly.

concepts.name: raphe nuclei
concepts.def: a moderate-size cluster of nuclei found in the brainstem. They function as autoreceptors in the brain and decrease the release of serotonin.
